task.name: Goal generation from discovery outputs
task.objective: Read validated discovery outputs from docs/architecture/*, generate a precise sequence of taskvisor goals. Each goal is atomic, testable, and has explicit dependencies. Goal IDs are 1-indexed: goal-001, goal-002, etc. This skill starts with a validation gate (Step 0) that checks cross-file consistency before any goal generation begins.

input.note: No $ARGUMENTS — reads docs/architecture/* files implicitly from the working directory. These files are produced by /tmux:task-plan-discover.

output.primary: goals.yaml with all generated goals — each goal has ID, description, depends_on, phase, acceptance criteria, and max_retries.
output.secondary: Per-goal goal.md files with Description, Dependencies, Deliverables, Acceptance Criteria, Test Environment reference, Context, Not In Scope, and Investigation Config sections.

glossary.term: Detected primary language slug (e.g. "php", "go", "python", "node"). Inherited from discovery session state, confirmed in docs/architecture/product-brief.md or project manifest.
Detected framework (e.g. "symfony", "laravel", "express", "gin"). Inherited from discovery session state, confirmed in docs/architecture/product-brief.md or project manifest.
Path to language-specific templates. Resolved via two-tier lookup: templates/<LANG>-<FRAMEWORK>/ first, then templates/<LANG>/, then templates/_base/. Inherited from discovery.
List of bounded context names extracted from docs/architecture/bounded-contexts.md level-3 headings under "Bounded Context Inventory". Built in Step 0.
Map of BC name to list of aggregate names. Extracted from bounded-contexts.md "Aggregates:" fields and cross-checked with domain-model.md headings. Built in Step 0.
Map of BC name to list of API endpoints. Extracted from docs/architecture/api-endpoints.md "Bounded Context" table cells. Built in Step 0.

Total number of goals to generate, calculated from the goal count formula: endpoints + auth_flows + listeners + cross_cutting + decisions + (aggregates * 2) + infrastructure. Computed in Step 1 (goal generation).

llm.mandate: NEVER generate goals if Step 0 validation fails — abort with actionable diagnostics
ALWAYS read discovery data from docs/architecture/* files on disk, never from conversation context (G-16)
NEVER modify discovery output files — generation is read-only on discovery outputs, write-only on goals
ALWAYS validate ALL consistency rules before reporting errors — collect every error, never stop at the first

step.title: Validate discovery outputs
step.rule: This step is a blocking gate. If any validation error is found, abort with a numbered error list and do NOT proceed to Step 1.

substep.title: File existence check
substep.action: Verify the following files and directories exist on disk using test -f / test -d:

For context-map.md: count the number of level-3 headings (### lines) under "Bounded Context Inventory" in bounded-contexts.md. If exactly one BC exists, skip the context-map.md check entirely (D-27). If two or more BCs exist, context-map.md is required.
For each missing required file, collect an error: "Missing required file: docs/architecture/<filename> — run /tmux:task-plan-discover first"
substep.rule: Do not stop at the first missing file. Check all files and collect all errors.

substep.title: Parse discovery files
substep.action: Read each discovery file and extract structured data:

parse.extract: Find all level-3 headings (### lines) under the "## Bounded Context Inventory" section. Each heading text is a BC name. Store as BC_LIST.
For each BC heading, find the "- **Aggregates:** " field. Parse the comma-separated values after the colon as aggregate names (trim whitespace). Store as AGGREGATE_MAP[bc_name] = [aggregate_names].

Find level-2 sections (## headings) — these are per-BC domain model files or sections. Under each, find level-3 headings (### lines) — these are aggregate names. Store for cross-checking with AGGREGATE_MAP.
For each aggregate, find "- Read model interface (query): " field. Store whether the value after the colon is empty or non-empty.
Find all "| Bounded Context | " table rows. The value in the second column is a BC name. Collect unique BC names referenced by endpoints. Store as ENDPOINT_MAP keys.

Find endpoints where Method is GET and Path has no trailing ID segment (e.g. /api/bookings vs /api/bookings/{id}), or where Description contains keywords "list", "search", or "index". These are collection/list actions. Tag each with its BC name.

Find and check non-emptiness of: base URL (line containing "Base URL" or "base_url"), test credentials (line containing "Test Credentials" or "test_credentials"), DB name (line containing "Test Database" or "DB name" or "db_name"), fixture command (line containing "Fixture Command" or "fixture_command"), Playwright status (line containing "Playwright" and "Status" or "playwright_status").

substep.title: Cross-file consistency checks
substep.action: Apply these 5 validation rules. Collect all errors — do not stop at the first failure.
substep.rule: Every BC name found in api-endpoints.md (from "Bounded Context" table cells) must have a matching ### heading in bounded-contexts.md under "Bounded Context Inventory". For each BC in api-endpoints.md that has no matching heading in bounded-contexts.md, collect error: "BC '<name>' found in api-endpoints.md but missing from bounded-contexts.md"

Every BC listed in bounded-contexts.md must have at least one aggregate in domain-model.md (at least one ### heading under that BC's section). For each BC with zero aggregates, collect error: "BC '<name>' has no aggregates in domain-model.md — every BC must have at least one"

Every BC that has GET endpoints with collection paths (list/search actions) in api-endpoints.md must have a non-empty "Read model interface (query):" field on at least one aggregate in domain-model.md. For each BC with list actions but no read model interface, collect error: "BC '<name>' has list/search actions but no read model interface noted in domain-model.md"

Every aggregate name listed in bounded-contexts.md under "- **Aggregates:** " must match a ### heading in domain-model.md for that BC. Comparison is case-insensitive and whitespace-trimmed. For each mismatch, collect error: "Aggregate name mismatch for BC '<bc_name>': bounded-contexts.md has '<name_a>', domain-model.md has '<name_b>'"

test-environment.md must exist and contain non-empty values for all of: base URL, test credentials, DB name, fixture command, Playwright status. For each missing or empty field, collect error: "test-environment.md missing required field: <field_name>"

substep.title: test-environment.md field validation
substep.action: This substep performs detailed field-by-field validation of test-environment.md. For each of the 5 required fields (base URL, test credentials, DB name, fixture command, Playwright status), verify:

checks.check: Value after the field label is not empty, not just whitespace, and looks like a URL (contains :// or starts with http/https).
Value after the field label is not empty — at minimum a username or token is specified.
Value after the field label is not empty and not just whitespace.
Value after the field label is not empty — a concrete command string is present.
Value after the field label is not empty and contains one of: "installed", "not installed", "not applicable", "needs", or equivalent status indicator.

substep.action: For each field that fails validation, collect error: "test-environment.md missing required field: <field_name>"

branch.action: Print all collected errors as a numbered list with file references:
ABORT. Do NOT proceed to Step 1.
Print: "Discovery output validation passed — {{bc_count}} bounded contexts, {{aggregate_count}} aggregates, {{endpoint_bc_count}} BCs with endpoints verified."
Store BC_LIST, AGGREGATE_MAP, and ENDPOINT_MAP for use in subsequent steps.
Proceed to Step 0b.

step.title: First-run Preconditions backfill (idempotent)
step.rule: Runs once per plan after Step 0 passes and BEFORE any goal generation. It brings PRE-EXISTING goal.md files (authored by an earlier plan that predates the Preconditions schema) up to the current schema so the taskvisor §5 auto-resume loop can re-evaluate env/infra-blocked goals.
Idempotent: a goal.md that already contains a "## Preconditions" heading is left UNTOUCHED — never write a duplicate section. A fresh project with no existing goal.md files is a no-op.
step.action: Enumerate existing .tmux-cli/goals/goal-*/goal.md files. For each file that does NOT already contain a "## Preconditions" heading, perform substeps 0b.1–0b.2.

substep.title: Derive the goal's preconditions
substep.action: FIRST, derive the DB signals locally. Step 0b runs BEFORE substep 1.2, so HAS_DATABASE/RUN_TARGET/SERVICES_EXTERNAL are NOT yet in scope — compute them HERE, do NOT forward-reference 1.2. Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md and apply the IDENTICAL rules as substep 1.2: RUN_TARGET=docker if "Run Target"==docker else local; SERVICES_EXTERNAL=true if "DB/services location"==external (default false in docker mode); HAS_DATABASE=true if it defines DB connection fields (host, port, user, password, db_name). If test-environment.md is absent, treat HAS_DATABASE=false.

Parse env/service gates for the goal from the environment-gate template (.tmux-cli/templates/{{LANG}}-{{FRAMEWORK}}/environment-gate.md, falling back to _base) and the project AGENTS.md runtime/preconditions section.
Map each gate to a line: "- [env] VAR_NAME — remedy" (required env var) or "- [service] host:port — remedy" (reachable TCP service).

DB sub-branch — applies to the Gate-0 / environment-gate goal ONLY (goal-001, phase=gate — the goal whose gates the environment-gate template defines), never to any other goal. If HAS_DATABASE AND (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL), ALSO append to that goal's derived list the two lines below, verbatim and byte-identical to WS1's fresh Gate-0 emission (substep 1.65):

lines.line: - [service] <host>:<port> — start/provision the test DB per docs/architecture/test-environment.md
- [env] DB_PASSWORD — set DB_PASSWORD from docs/architecture/test-environment.md (do not inline the value)

substep.rule: <host>/<port> MUST be substituted with the literal values from test-environment.md (e.g. 127.0.0.1 and 5432). NEVER emit shell-variable syntax ($DB_HOST/$DB_PORT) and NEVER inline the password — the [env] spec is the bare variable NAME (DB_PASSWORD), which the daemon checks via os.LookupEnv (GM-05). The daemon TCP-dials the literal [service] spec, so shell-variable syntax would never resolve.
substep.action: In docker/compose-internal mode (RUN_TARGET=docker AND NOT SERVICES_EXTERNAL), add NEITHER line — the compose-internal DB is provisioned by Scaffold (goal-002), so no Gate-0 ops precondition is required.
If no gate applies to this goal, use the single literal line "none".

substep.title: Inject the section, preserving all other content
substep.action: Insert a "## Preconditions" section (the derived list, or "none") into goal.md immediately BEFORE the "## Not In Scope" heading; if that heading is absent, append the section at end of file.
substep.rule: PRESERVE every other section verbatim — Description, Dependencies, Deliverables, Acceptance Criteria, Validation, Context, Investigation Config, Not In Scope. Only the new "## Preconditions" section is added.

step.title: Generate Gate 0 goal (goal-001)
step.rule: Step 0 must have passed before this step executes. If Step 0 aborted, do NOT run Step 1.
Gate 0 is always goal-001 with phase=gate. This is hardcoded, not computed.

substep.title: Resolve environment-gate template
substep.action: Attempt to read the language-specific overlay template first, then fall back to base:
Store the resolved template content as GATE_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used (overlay or base).
substep.rule: Checks MUST be loaded from the resolved template, not hardcoded in this XML (E-10).
substep.action: Resolve the AGENTS.md template the same overlay→base way, so the AGENTS.md bootstrap (substep 1.5) has a defined source:
Store the resolved template content as AGENTS_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used (overlay or base).

substep.title: Read project state for conditional checks
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md. Determine:
substep.check: test-environment.md "Run Target" == docker → set RUN_TARGET=docker, else RUN_TARGET=local.
test-environment.md "DB/services location" == external → set SERVICES_EXTERNAL=true (default false in docker mode).
test-environment.md defines DB connection fields (host, port, user, password, db_name). If yes, set HAS_DATABASE=true.
test-environment.md Playwright status is "installed" or "needs". If yes, set HAS_FRONTEND=true.
substep.action: Scan docs/architecture/adrs/ directory. Determine:
substep.check: Any ADR selects Redis or Memcached as a caching/session backend. If yes, set HAS_REDIS=true.
Any ADR selects RabbitMQ or async Messenger transport. If yes, set HAS_RABBITMQ=true.
substep.action: Set HAS_SERVICES = HAS_REDIS OR HAS_RABBITMQ.
Read composer.json to extract PHP version constraint from require.php and any ext-* requirements beyond the template defaults (pdo_pgsql, intl, mbstring).

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria from GE-01..GE-13
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array from the spec criteria (docs/task-plan-spec.md:469-488), filtered by project conditionals:

criteria.item: Each check targets only system-level deps relevant to this project (E-01)
Each check runs independently — one failure does not skip other checks (E-02)
Missing dep recorded in correction with exact install command (E-03)
Gate 0 does NOT check Composer dev-dependencies: PHPStan, ECS, Deptrac, PHPUnit (E-04)
Gate 0 does NOT create quality tool config files: phpstan.neon, deptrac.yaml, ecs.php (E-05)
Gate 0 checks PHP extensions required by the project (E-06)
Gate 0 verifies Docker engine + Compose v2 + running daemon, can pull+run a container (E-14)
Host PHP/extension checks deferred to in-container scaffold — not run on host (E-15)
After correction, Gate 0 re-runs failed checks and any checks whose inputs changed; on the final cycle before overall pass, re-run all checks for end-to-end verification. (E-07)
Gate 0 passes only when ALL checks are green (E-08)
No subsequent goal dispatched while Gate 0 is pending/failed (E-09)
Check list generated from templates, not hardcoded (E-10)
Playwright system check verifies Node/npm presence only, not Playwright package (E-12)
Correction ordering: system packages → extensions → services → frontend (E-13)
Gate 0 writes a valid AGENTS.md at project root containing all goal-001 results: run target, verified runtime/extensions/Composer, DB + services config, base URL, gate status (E-16)

substep.rule: Include conditional criteria (E-12) only when its condition is true for this project.

E-06 and host-extension criteria render in RUN_TARGET=local mode only; E-14/E-15 render in RUN_TARGET=docker mode only; E-16 is always included (both modes). In local mode the array is identical to today plus the always-on E-16.

substep.title: Compose validation commands from template
substep.action: Extract executable shell commands from GATE_TEMPLATE check_command fields. Build VALIDATE array:

commands.cmd: grep -q "<!-- GATE0:BEGIN -->" AGENTS.md && grep -q "## Run Target" AGENTS.md && grep -q "## Gate 0 Status" AGENTS.md

commands.condition: RUN_TARGET=local AND extra_extensions from composer.json ext-*

substep.rule: ENV-A01/ENV-A02 are always-on (both modes): they assert Gate 0 wrote a valid AGENTS.md. ENV-A02 greps the EXACT sentinel/heading strings the agents.md template writes — `<!-- GATE0:BEGIN -->`, `## Run Target`, `## Gate 0 Status` — do not alter these strings (contract with the agents.md template).

In RUN_TARGET=docker mode, host PHP-world checks (ENV-R01/P01/X*) and host frontend checks (ENV-F01/F02) are deferred to the in-container scaffold (E-15); the docker block (ENV-C01..C04) proves the host can actually run containers.
DB credential variables ($DB_HOST etc.) reference values from docs/architecture/test-environment.md — never inline passwords (GM-05).
Never include vendor/bin/* commands — those are Scaffold goal-002 (E-04).

Every validate command MUST be satisfiable by the implementer inside its sandbox (a local binary, file, or already-present extension). A rule requiring a LIVE external service, network reachability, or a runtime secret is NOT a validate rule — emit it as a ## Preconditions line ([service] host:port or [env] VAR) for the daemon to gate, never in validate or acceptance.

substep.title: Detect bindings and build FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS (C8 spec-defect guard)
substep.action: Reuse C3's binding parse: scan the project-root AGENTS.md and docs/architecture/adrs/ for the binding keywords in binding-forbidden-map below. Treat an ABSENT AGENTS.md AND absent adrs/ as "no binding" — FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS is empty and every reference is allowed; this is a clean no-op, never an error or a block.
For every detected binding keyword, union its forbidden-artifact patterns into FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS. Multiple bindings union their sets; a keyword that matches no row adds nothing (safe no-op).

binding-forbidden-map.note: Documented + extensible: exactly one <binding> row per keyword. Match patterns CONSERVATIVELY (basename / path-suffix) so a doc that merely mentions the word is not flagged — only a real artifact reference is. Adding a new <binding> row takes effect at runtime with NO Go code change (this block is DATA, read at runtime).

substep.rule: FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS is consumed by the guard rule in substep 1.5 (investigation-config section) and is the SAME contract the investigate.xml and investigate-worker.xml preflights re-check at validation time. A binding contradiction is ALWAYS a spec-defect owned by the planner — never an implementer code defect.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Gate 0 verifies system-level prerequisites for the chosen run target (docker|local) before any code generation, and bootstraps a valid project-root AGENTS.md from the verified results. All checks run independently — a single failure does not skip remaining checks. See docs/architecture/test-environment.md for the run target, DB credentials, and test environment configuration.

section.name: investigation-config
section.action: Build 2-4 investigators based on RUN_TARGET and project conditionals. The two mandatory investigators are run-target-specific: local mode uses Core Runtime + PHP Extensions; docker mode uses Docker Engine & Daemon + Compose Readiness.

investigator.name: Core Runtime
investigator.pass: All commands exit 0, PHP version satisfies composer.json constraint
investigator.fail: Missing binary or version mismatch

investigator.name: PHP Extensions
investigator.commands: php -m | grep -i pdo_pgsql, php -m | grep -i intl, php -m | grep -i mbstring (+ project-specific ext-*)
investigator.pass: All required extensions present in php -m output
investigator.fail: One or more extensions missing

investigator.name: Docker Engine & Daemon
investigator.commands: docker --version, docker info, docker run --rm hello-world
investigator.pass: Engine installed, daemon up, can pull+run a container
investigator.fail: Docker missing, daemon down, or cannot run a container

investigator.name: Compose Readiness
investigator.pass: Compose v2 available. Also writes/replaces the AGENTS.md GATE0 block from verified results (see AGENTS.md Bootstrap).
investigator.fail: Compose v2 missing

investigator.condition: HAS_SERVICES AND (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL) — Redis or RabbitMQ selected in ADRs; in docker mode only when the service is marked external
investigator.name: Service Dependencies
investigator.commands: redis-cli ping (if Redis), rabbitmqctl status (if RabbitMQ)
investigator.pass: All selected services respond
investigator.fail: Service unreachable or not running

section.rule: RUN_TARGET=local emits Core Runtime + PHP Extensions; RUN_TARGET=docker emits Docker Engine & Daemon + Compose Readiness. Exactly one of these pairs is the mandatory floor — the two are mutually exclusive by RUN_TARGET.

Include Service Dependencies only when HAS_SERVICES=true AND (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL). In docker mode with compose-internal services these are skipped (the compose stack does not exist until Scaffold). DB reachability/auth is no longer an investigator — it is emitted as a ## Preconditions line (substep 1.65) for the daemon to gate.

Investigator-floor holds per branch (GM-08): local = Core Runtime + PHP Extensions (+ Services) = 2-4; docker = Docker Engine + Compose (+ external Services) = 2-4. Both satisfy minimum 2 / maximum 4.
Each investigator has type, file paths, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

C8 spec-defect guard: before writing any <files> or <commands> artifact reference into an investigator, match it (basename / path-suffix) against FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS from substep 1.45. On a match, DO NOT write the reference — write a marker comment in its place instead: <!-- SPEC-DEFECT: <artifact> forbidden by <binding> binding -->. With no binding detected, FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS is empty and every reference is written normally (no-op).

substep.section: AGENTS.md Bootstrap (E-16, ENV-A01/ENV-A02 — a deliverable of the Compose Readiness / Core Runtime investigator): 1. Resolve AGENTS_TEMPLATE (substep 1.1 overlay→base lookup). 2. Fill it from the verified gate results: run target (and DB/services location if docker), runtime/extensions/Composer or container runtime versions, DB + services config, base URL, gate status ({{passed_count}}/{{total_count}}). 3. Write the filled block to AGENTS.md at the PROJECT ROOT. The block is delimited by the sentinels `<!-- GATE0:BEGIN -->` … `<!-- GATE0:END -->`. 4. Idempotent replace: if `<!-- GATE0:BEGIN -->`…`<!-- GATE0:END -->` already exists, replace everything between the sentinels (inclusive); else append the block. NEVER touch content outside the sentinels (later goals and any human-authored sections are preserved). 5. Guard: if a pre-existing AGENTS.md has NO GATE0 sentinels, append the block — never overwrite the human file.

Correction ordering protocol (E-13): when multiple failures are detected, apply corrections in this order: 0. Container runtime — Docker engine, daemon, can-run-container, Compose (ENV-C01..C04) — only if RUN_TARGET=docker 1. System packages — PHP CLI, Composer (ENV-R01, ENV-P01) — only if RUN_TARGET=local 2. PHP extensions — pdo_pgsql, intl, mbstring + project-specific (ENV-X01..XN) — only if RUN_TARGET=local 3. Services — Redis, RabbitMQ (ENV-S01, ENV-S02) — only if selected and (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL) 4. Frontend — Node.js, npm (ENV-F01, ENV-F02) — only if Playwright/frontend detected and (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL)

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Composer dev-dependencies (PHPStan, ECS, Deptrac, PHPUnit), quality tool configs (phpstan.neon, deptrac.yaml, ecs.php), Playwright npm package — all handled by Scaffold (goal-002).
substep.rule: AGENTS.md is deliberately IN scope as a results artifact (E-16); the no-config-file rule (E-05) covers only quality-tool configs (phpstan.neon, deptrac.yaml, ecs.php), not AGENTS.md.

substep.title: Compose Gate-0 preconditions
substep.action: Build the PRECONDITIONS list — the daemon-gated ops requirements an in-sandbox implementer CANNOT satisfy (a live DB server plus its secret). These are NOT validate rules: they are emitted to the goal's ## Preconditions section so the daemon parks goal-001 until each is met (evaluatePreconditions TCP-dials each [service] spec and os.LookupEnv each [env] spec).

branch.action: Resolve the LITERAL DB host and port from docs/architecture/test-environment.md — the actual values the doc declares (e.g. 127.0.0.1 and 5432), NOT the shell variables $DB_HOST/$DB_PORT. The daemon TCP-dials the literal [service] spec, so shell-variable syntax would never resolve.
Set PRECONDITIONS to these two lines (reuse the per-goal `- [kind] spec — remedy` format):

lines.line: - [service] <host>:<port> — start/provision the test DB per docs/architecture/test-environment.md
- [env] DB_PASSWORD — set DB_PASSWORD from docs/architecture/test-environment.md (do not inline the value)

branch.rule: <host>/<port> MUST be substituted with the literal values from test-environment.md. NEVER emit shell-variable syntax ($DB_HOST/$DB_PORT) and NEVER inline the password — the [env] spec is the bare variable NAME (DB_PASSWORD), which the daemon checks via os.LookupEnv (GM-05).

branch.condition: otherwise (no DB, or docker compose-internal DB)
branch.action: Set PRECONDITIONS = none. Compose-internal DB/services are provisioned by Scaffold (goal-002), so no ops precondition is required at Gate 0.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Verify environment prerequisites for the chosen run target (docker|local)
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 1.3 (12-15 items depending on run target and conditionals)
VALIDATE array from substep 1.4
CONTEXT string from substep 1.5 (purpose + correction ordering)
Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 1.5's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 1.6
PRECONDITIONS from substep 1.65 (array of {kind,spec,remedy}; goal-create persists them to BOTH goals.yaml and goal.md's ## Preconditions section). Pass nothing/empty when PRECONDITIONS=none.

SCOPE array — see the <scope-derivation> rule below. Gate 0 touches repo-root skeleton files (no src/ BC footprint) and is the FIRST goal with no concurrent sibling, so OMIT scope (unknown ⇒ serialized, which is correct for the bootstrap gate).

substep.scope-derivation: The `scope` param is the goal's declared file footprint (array of path globs / namespace prefixes) that the daemon's disjoint-scope co-scheduling gate reads to decide whether two goals may run CONCURRENTLY under MaxGoals>1. DERIVE it from the goal's Deliverables file paths: emit the distinct directory globs that cover every deliverable (e.g. deliverables under src/Booking/Domain/ ⇒ scope ["src/Booking/Domain/**"]; a single file ⇒ that file path). CONSERVATIVE CONTRACT: overlap OR an omitted/unknown scope ⇒ the daemon SERIALIZES the goals (never co-schedules), so it is always safe to omit when the footprint is unclear. An explicit author-provided `scope:` overrides derivation. The runtime reads ONLY this persisted field — it never parses goal.md — so scope MUST be passed here to enable safe co-scheduling.
substep.rule: Description is 73 characters — well within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=5 because Gate 0 has 4 independent failure categories that may each need a correction cycle.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Gate 0 created: goal-001 (phase=gate) with {{investigator_count}} investigators, max_retries=5"
Proceed to Step 2 (when added by subsequent tasks).

step.title: Generate Scaffold goal (goal-002)
step.rule: Step 1 must have completed successfully (goal-001 created) before this step executes.
Scaffold is always goal-002 with phase=scaffold, depends_on=[goal-001] (G-02).
All inputs are read from docs/architecture/* files and templates on disk — never from conversation context (G-16).

substep.title: Resolve scaffold template
substep.action: Attempt to read the language-specific overlay template first, then fall back to base:
Store the resolved template content as SCAFFOLD_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used (overlay or base).
substep.rule: Directory structure and Share namespace layout MUST come from the resolved template, not hardcoded in this XML.

substep.title: Read architecture inputs
substep.action: Read the following files and extract structured data:

read.extract: All level-3 headings under "## Bounded Context Inventory" — these are the BC names for DDD directory structure and Deptrac layers.
Composer package names from the file. If file does not exist, fall back to .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/libraries.md.
.env.test variable names and values for SC-10.

substep.action: Read Deptrac layer template from .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/bounded-contexts.md (lines 79-128) for DomainAndShare composite layer pattern and ruleset.
Read quality tool configs from .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/quality-gates.md for PHPStan level 9, ECS, and Deptrac enforcement commands.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (SC-01..SC-13)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 13 scaffold criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:420-434:

criteria.item: composer.json contains all packages from docs/architecture/ decisions
composer install completes without errors (exit code 0)
DDD directory structure exists for every BC from discovery: src/{BC}/Domain/, src/{BC}/Application/, src/{BC}/Infrastructure/
Share namespace exists: src/Share/ with DataType, Event, Exception, Messaging subdirectories
bin/console boots without errors (exit code 0)
vendor/bin/phpunit runs with zero tests, zero errors (exit code 0)
vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/ --level=9 passes on empty project (exit code 0)
vendor/bin/ecs check src/ passes on empty project (exit code 0)
vendor/bin/deptrac analyse passes with no violations on empty structure (exit code 0)
.env.test exists with variables matching docs/architecture/test-environment.md
phpunit.xml has separate unit and integration test suites configured
Deptrac config has DDD layers: Domain (depends on nothing + Share), Application (depends on DomainAndShare), Infrastructure (depends on DomainAndShare + Application), Share (depends on nothing)
Deptrac config groups Domain + Share into {BC}DomainAndShare composite layer (previo2 pattern using bool collector with must/must_not)

substep.rule: All 13 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with 6 CLI commands that verify the scaffold is functional:
substep.rule: These are Composer dev-dependency commands — Gate 0 (goal-001) must have verified that Composer itself is installed before scaffold runs.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config and fan-out hints
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Scaffold creates a bootable, TDD-ready Symfony skeleton with DDD directory structure, quality tools, and Share namespace. All BC names come from docs/architecture/bounded-contexts.md. Composer packages from docs/architecture/libraries.md. Deptrac layer definitions and {BC}DomainAndShare composite from .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/bounded-contexts.md. Quality tool configs (PHPStan level 9, ECS, Deptrac) from .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/quality-gates.md. Test environment variables from docs/architecture/test-environment.md.

section.name: fan-out-hints
section.action: Build fan-out hints for multi-task execution (3 file-disjoint tasks):

task.name: Composer setup
task.scope: composer.json with all packages + composer install

task.name: DDD directory structure
task.scope: Create DDD directories per BC + Share namespace

section.rule: File-disjoint: task-0 owns composer root, task-1 owns src/*/, task-2 owns config root files. Zero overlap.
SC-05 (bin/console boots) is verified after ALL tasks complete — it depends on both Composer packages and Symfony kernel config.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Composer check
investigator.pass: Exit 0 — composer.json is valid and all dependencies resolved
investigator.fail: Invalid composer.json or unresolved dependencies

investigator.name: Kernel boot
investigator.pass: Exit 0 — Symfony kernel boots successfully
investigator.fail: Kernel boot error or missing service configuration

investigator.name: Quality tools
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check src/ && vendor/bin/deptrac analyse
investigator.pass: All three commands exit 0 — zero errors, zero violations
investigator.fail: Any tool reports errors or violations

investigator.name: Test runner
investigator.pass: Exit 0, zero errors — test suites discovered and run successfully
investigator.fail: Test discovery failure, errors, or non-zero exit

section.rule: Minimum 4 investigators for scaffold (GM-08). Each has type, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Application code, domain logic, controller actions, fixtures, E2E tests, Playwright setup — all handled by subsequent goals (goal-003+).

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Create bootable TDD-ready Symfony skeleton with DDD structure, quality tools, and Share namespace
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 2.3 (exactly 13 items, SC-01..SC-13)
VALIDATE array from substep 2.4 (6 commands)
CONTEXT string from substep 2.5 (purpose + fan-out hints)
Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 2.5's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 2.6

SCOPE array — see the <scope-derivation> rule in substep 1.7. Scaffold lays down the repo-wide skeleton (config/, composer.json, src/Share/**, quality-tool configs) and runs before any BC goal, so OMIT scope (unknown ⇒ serialized — correct for the skeleton goal).

substep.rule: Description is 97 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 — scaffold has fewer independent failure categories than Gate 0.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Scaffold created: goal-002 (phase=scaffold, depends_on=[goal-001]) with 4 investigators, 3 fan-out tasks, max_retries=3"
Proceed to Step 3 (when added by subsequent tasks).

step.title: Generate domain layer goals (one per BC)
step.rule: Step 2 must have completed successfully (goal-002 created) before this step executes.
Exactly one goal per BC. phase=domain. depends_on=[goal-002]. Goal IDs are sequential starting from goal-003.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files and templates on disk — never from conversation context (G-16).

substep.title: Resolve domain templates
substep.action: Attempt to read language-specific overlay templates first, then fall back to base:
Store resolved templates as DOMAIN_TEMPLATE, CONTEXT_MAP_TEMPLATE, SERVICE_CONTRACTS_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used for each.
substep.rule: DDD patterns (aggregate roots, VOs, events, ACL) MUST come from resolved templates, not hardcoded in this XML.

substep.title: Read architecture inputs for domain generation
substep.action: Read the following files and extract structured data:

read.extract: Use BC_LIST from Step 0 (already validated). Each BC name maps to aggregates via AGGREGATE_MAP.

For each BC, extract: aggregate roots (### headings under BC section), value objects per aggregate, domain events per aggregate, domain services, repository interface descriptions, read model interface presence, invariants.
For each BC, extract: upstream BCs it depends on, relationship type (ACL, Published Language, Shared Kernel), event contracts, ID VO references.

substep.rule: Single-BC projects skip context-mapping.md reading (D-27). Single-BC goals have no ACL interfaces.

substep.title: Initialize Share type ownership tracker
substep.action: Create an in-memory map SHARE_OWNERSHIP = {} to track which BC's goal owns each Share file.
As BCs are processed in order, the first BC that introduces a shared type claims it. Subsequent BCs reference it but do NOT include it in their deliverables.
Tracked files: src/Share/DomainEventTrait.php, src/Share/Exception/DomainException.php, src/Share/DataType/*.php, src/Share/Event/*.php
substep.rule: DomainEventTrait and base DomainException are always owned by the FIRST BC processed — they go into task-0 of that BC's goal.

substep.title: Iterate BCs and generate domain goals
substep.action: For each BC in BC_LIST (order from bounded-contexts.md), execute substeps 3.14.4a through 3.14.4i:
Maintain a running GOAL_INDEX starting at 3 (goal-003 is the first domain goal).

substep.title: Extract BC aggregates
substep.action: From domain-model.md, extract for this BC:
substep.field: AGGREGATES — list of aggregate root names (### headings under this BC)
VOS_PER_AGGREGATE — map of aggregate name to list of value object names
EVENTS_PER_AGGREGATE — map of aggregate name to list of domain event names
DOMAIN_SERVICES — list of domain service names (if any span multiple aggregates)
READ_MODEL_INTERFACES — list of read model interfaces (query side, Application layer)
INVARIANTS — per aggregate, the business rules enforced
substep.action: Set AGGREGATE_COUNT = len(AGGREGATES).

substep.title: Extract cross-BC dependencies
substep.action: From context-mapping.md (if multi-BC), extract for this BC:
substep.field: PUBLISHED_EVENTS — list of {event_name, producer_bc, consumer_bcs} where this BC is producer or consumer
FOREIGN_ID_VOS — list of ID value objects for cross-BC references
substep.action: For single-BC projects: UPSTREAM_DEPS=[], PUBLISHED_EVENTS=[], FOREIGN_ID_VOS=[].

substep.title: Collect deliverable file paths
substep.action: Build DELIVERABLES list for this BC:
For Share types introduced by this BC (not already in SHARE_OWNERSHIP):
If this is the FIRST BC processed AND DomainEventTrait not yet in SHARE_OWNERSHIP:
Update SHARE_OWNERSHIP for all newly claimed Share files.

branch.action: Build multi-task fan-out:

task.name: Shared infrastructure (prerequisite)
task.scope: DomainEventTrait (if owned by this BC), base DomainException (if owned), Share DataTypes introduced by this BC, Share Events produced by this BC, ACL interfaces (cross-BC deps)
task.owns: All src/Share/ files owned by this BC + src/{{BC}}/Domain/ACL/*.php
task.rule: If this is NOT the first BC and DomainEventTrait/DomainException are already owned, task-0 still exists for BC-specific Share types and ACL interfaces. If task-0 would be empty (no Share types, no ACL interfaces), skip task-0 and make all aggregate tasks independent.

task.name: Per-aggregate task (parallel)
task.scope: One aggregate root, its VOs, its events, its repo interface, its unit tests

branch.action: Assign domain services to the aggregate task with the strongest dependency, or to task-0 if service spans all aggregates.
Assign read model interfaces to the aggregate task whose query contract they serve.
branch.rule: File-disjoint rule: verify zero overlap between task deliverable sets. If overlap found, move shared file to task-0.

branch.action: Single task — all deliverables in one task.

task.scope: All deliverables for this BC's domain layer

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria from PD-01..PD-20
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array, filtering PD-01..PD-20 to BC-relevant items:

criteria.item: Aggregate root class has private constructor — verify via PHPStan rule or AST check on src/{{BC}}/Domain/{{Aggregate}}.php
Aggregate root has at least one named constructor (public static factory method returning self)
All value objects are final class — grep src/{{BC}}/Domain/ValueObject/ for class without final
Value object constructor validates input, throws DomainException on invalid state — unit test per VO
Value objects implement equals() method — grep VO files for equals
Domain events named in past tense (e.g. {{Aggregate}}Created, not Create{{Aggregate}})
Repository interface has domain-specific methods (findByEmail, nextIdentity), not generic find($id)
Read model interface exists in src/{{BC}}/Application/ReadModel/, returns DTOs
Zero imports from Infrastructure or Application namespace in Domain files — deptrac or grep check
Unit test for creation via each aggregate's named constructor exists (per aggregate)
Unit tests for each mutation method exist (one per state-changing method on each aggregate)
Event-generation assertion exists per aggregate (each domain event asserted recorded by a test)

DECOMPOSE PER INVARIANT — for EACH invariant/uniqueness/existence rule named in the {{BC}} domain model, emit ONE atomic criterion of the form: "{{Invariant}} invariant — {{Invariant}}Checker domain service enforces it AND a test asserts {{Invariant}}Exception is thrown when violated". Name the checker class (src/{{BC}}/Domain/Service/) and the exception thrown-test (tests/Unit/{{BC}}/Domain/) so each is independently greppable. NEVER collapse these into a single 'tests cover invariant violations' line.
Unit tests run green — vendor/bin/phpunit --filter={{BC}}\\Domain passes
PHPStan level 9 passes on Domain namespace — vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/{{BC}}/Domain
No Doctrine annotations/attributes on domain objects — grep for @ORM\ and #[ORM\ (must be zero)
Domain services exist for cross-aggregate operations
Cross-BC references use ID value objects, not entity imports
ACL interfaces defined in src/{{BC}}/Domain/ACL/ for each upstream dependency
DomainEventTrait implemented with collect-only pattern — events not dispatched from aggregate
Shared types live in src/Share/, not duplicated per BC
Cross-BC event DTOs live in src/Share/Event/ as Published Language
Aggregate sizing follows one-aggregate-per-transaction — prefer small aggregates referencing by ID

substep.rule: Include conditional criteria only when their condition is true for this BC.
Replace {{BC}} and {{Aggregate}} placeholders with actual BC and aggregate names from discovery.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with CLI commands that verify the domain layer:

commands.cmd: grep -rn 'use App\\{{BC}}\\Infrastructure\|use App\\{{BC}}\\Application' src/{{BC}}/Domain/ | grep -v 'Application\\ReadModel' | wc -l (must output 0)

For EACH {{BC}} invariant emit a presence pair (GM-09b/GM-09c): grep -rl 'class {{Invariant}}Checker' src/{{BC}}/Domain/Service/ (must return ≥1) and grep -rlE '{{Invariant}}(AlreadyInUse|NotFound|Invalid)Exception' tests/Unit/{{BC}}/Domain/ (must return ≥1)

substep.rule: The PD-09 grep excludes Application\ReadModel imports which are allowed for read model interface references from Domain services. All other Application/Infrastructure imports are violations.

GM-09b/GM-09c: PD-10 invariant coverage is gated by the per-invariant presence-greps above, NOT by a coverage judgment on a green suite. Emit one PD-10-INV pair per invariant named in the {{BC}} domain model — the same set decomposed into the PD-10-INV acceptance items. A green suite with a missing presence-grep is a FAIL.
Replace {{BC}} with the actual BC namespace name from discovery.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Domain layer for {{BC}}: implements aggregate roots, value objects, domain events, repository interfaces, and unit tests following DDD patterns. All domain model details come from docs/architecture/domain-model.md ({{BC}} section). Cross-BC dependencies from docs/architecture/context-mapping.md. DDD patterns from resolved domain template ({{DOMAIN_TEMPLATE tier}}). ACL patterns from resolved context-mapping template ({{CONTEXT_MAP_TEMPLATE tier}}).

section.name: fan-out-hints
section.action: Include the fan-out structure from substep 3.14.4d — task-0 (shared) followed by task-1..N (per aggregate) for multi-aggregate BCs, or single task for single-aggregate BCs.
List each task's owned files explicitly. State the depends_on chain.
section.rule: File-disjoint rule: parallel tasks have zero overlapping deliverable files. State this explicitly.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Unit test runner + invariant coverage
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpunit --filter={{BC}}\\Domain; AND for EACH {{BC}} invariant a presence pair: grep -rl 'class {{Invariant}}Checker' src/{{BC}}/Domain/Service/ and grep -rlE '{{Invariant}}(AlreadyInUse|NotFound|Invalid)Exception' tests/Unit/{{BC}}/Domain/
investigator.pass: phpunit exits 0 and is non-empty (test count "OK (N tests"/"N tests", NOT "No tests executed!"), all green; AND every invariant presence-grep returns ≥1 line
investigator.fail: Non-zero/empty phpunit run ("No tests executed!", any failure/error), OR any invariant presence-grep returns 0 — an enforced invariant that lacks its Service checker class or its thrown-exception test is a FAIL even when the suite is green (GM-09b: objective gate, never a coverage judgment)

investigator.name: Static analysis
investigator.pass: Exit 0, zero errors at level 9
investigator.fail: Type errors, missing return types, incorrect signatures

investigator.name: Layer violation check
investigator.pass: Zero matches for both greps — no infrastructure leakage, no Doctrine annotations
investigator.fail: Infrastructure imports or Doctrine annotations found in Domain namespace

investigator.name: DDD convention check
investigator.commands: grep -r 'private function __construct' src/{{BC}}/Domain/ (must match aggregate count), grep -rn 'final class' src/{{BC}}/Domain/ValueObject/ (must match VO count), grep -rn 'function equals' src/{{BC}}/Domain/ValueObject/ (must match VO count)
investigator.pass: Private constructors on all aggregates, final on all VOs, equals() on all VOs
investigator.fail: Missing private constructor, non-final VO, missing equals()

section.rule: Minimum 3 investigators (Unit tests + Static analysis + Layer violation). Investigator 4 added when AGGREGATE_COUNT > 1 or cross-BC deps exist (GM-08).
Each investigator has type, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

section.name: template-references
section.action: Include references to resolved templates so the executing agent can read DDD patterns:

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Doctrine XML mappings, infrastructure repository implementations, command/query handlers, controllers, API endpoints, database migrations, fixtures, E2E tests, Messenger transport config, ACL adapters (Infrastructure layer) — all handled by subsequent Application/Infrastructure goals.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Implement {{BC}} domain: aggregates, VOs, events, repo interfaces (≤120 chars — truncate aggregate list if needed)
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.14.4e (10-20 items depending on BC conditionals)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.14.4f (4 commands)
CONTEXT string from substep 3.14.4g (purpose + fan-out hints + template refs)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.14.4g's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 3.14.4h
goal-002

SCOPE array — see the <scope-derivation> rule in substep 1.7. Derive from this BC's domain Deliverables (substep 3.14.4c): emit ["src/{{BC}}/Domain/**"] (add any extra distinct deliverable dirs). A focused single-BC domain footprint lets the gate co-schedule this goal with a DIFFERENT BC's goal under MaxGoals>1.

substep.rule: Description must be ≤120 chars. If BC name + aggregate list exceeds limit, use "Implement {{BC}} domain layer: {{N}} aggregates, VOs, events, interfaces".
max_retries=3 — domain goals have focused scope within a single BC.

check.action: Print error with full context. Continue to next BC (do not abort entire step for one BC failure).
Print: "Domain goal created: goal-{{GOAL_INDEX:03d}} (phase=domain, BC={{BC}}, depends_on=[goal-002]) with {{investigator_count}} investigators, {{task_count}} fan-out tasks, max_retries=3"
Increment GOAL_INDEX.

substep.title: Report domain goal generation results
substep.action: After all BCs processed, print summary:

check.action: Print warning: "{{bc_count - domain_goal_count}} BC(s) failed goal creation — review errors above."

substep.action: Proceed to Step 3.15 (Application layer goals).

step.title: Per-BC application layer goals
step.rule: Per-BC domain goals MUST exist before this step runs. Each application goal depends_on its own BC's domain goal (G-04).
One goal per BC, phase=application. Never batch multiple BCs into one goal.
All inputs are read from docs/architecture/* files and templates on disk — never from conversation context (G-16).
description max 120 chars — detail belongs in acceptance/validate arrays.

substep.title: Resolve service-contracts template
substep.action: Attempt to read the language-specific overlay template first, then fall back to base:
Store the resolved template content as SERVICE_CONTRACTS_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used.
substep.rule: Handler injection patterns and read model interface conventions MUST come from the resolved template, not hardcoded (G-16).

substep.title: Read architecture inputs
substep.action: Read the following files and extract structured data for the application layer loop:

read.extract: All level-3 headings under "## Bounded Context Inventory" — the BC names.
For each BC, the comma-separated aggregate names from "- **Aggregates:** " field.

For each BC, partition endpoints into: - COMMANDS: POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE methods → command handlers (mutations) - QUERIES: GET methods → query handlers (reads) Store as USE_CASE_MAP[bc_name] = { commands: [...], queries: [...] }
GET endpoints with collection paths (no trailing ID segment) or descriptions containing "list"/"search"/"index". Tag each with BC name for read model interface detection.
For each BC and aggregate, extract the repository interface name pattern: {Aggregate}RepositoryInterface. Also check "Read model interface (query):" fields for read model interface names.

substep.action: For each BC, compute HANDLER_COUNT = len(commands) + len(queries).
For each BC with LIST_ACTIONS, note that ReadModelInterface + DTO + Criteria files are needed.

substep.title: Determine domain goal IDs for depends_on
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter all goal entries where phase=domain. Map each domain goal to its BC name (from the goal description or context).
For each BC in BC_LIST, find its corresponding domain goal ID. Store as APP_DEPENDS_ON[bc_name] = domain_goal_id.

check.condition: any BC in BC_LIST has no matching domain goal
check.action: ABORT with error: "BC '{{bc_name}}' has no domain goal in goals.yaml — run domain goal generation first"

substep.rule: Each application goal depends_on ONLY its own BC's domain goal — not other BCs' goals (G-04).
Domain goal IDs MUST be dynamically read from goals.yaml, never hardcoded or computed from position.

substep.title: Loop: compose and create application goals per BC
substep.action: For each BC in BC_LIST, execute substeps 3.15.4a through 3.15.4f:

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (PA-01..PA-12)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 12 application layer criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:564-577, parameterized with BC name and handler count:

criteria.item: Each use case from api-endpoints.md has exactly one handler class ({{HANDLER_COUNT}} handlers for {{BC_NAME}})
Handlers inject repository interfaces (ports), not implementations — constructor params use {{Aggregate}}RepositoryInterface type hints
Zero imports from Infrastructure namespace in src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ files
Zero imports from HTTP/Controller namespace in src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ files
Command DTOs have no business logic — only public properties or constructor, no methods with domain operations
Command handlers __invoke() returns void or scalar ID type
Query handlers __invoke() returns DTO objects, never entities or aggregate roots
Unit tests mock repository/read-model interfaces, not Doctrine implementations
Unit tests cover: happy path, validation failure, domain exception propagation per handler
vendor/bin/phpunit --filter={{BC_NAME}}\\Application passes (exit 0, all green)
vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ --level=9 passes (exit 0, zero errors)
vendor/bin/deptrac analyse --filter={{BC_NAME}} passes — Application depends only on Domain + Share

substep.rule: All 12 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with 3 CLI commands scoped to this BC's Application namespace:
substep.rule: Validation commands are BC-scoped — not project-wide. Each command verifies one of PA-10, PA-11, PA-12.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config and fan-out hints
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Application layer for {{BC_NAME}}: command/query handlers orchestrate domain operations via ports. Use cases from docs/architecture/api-endpoints.md ({{BC_NAME}} section). Handler injection patterns from {{SERVICE_CONTRACTS_TEMPLATE_TIER}} template (service-contracts.md). Domain interfaces (ports) defined in own BC's domain goal deliverables. Command handlers: receive Command DTO, invoke aggregate via repository interface, return void/scalar ID. Query handlers: receive Query DTO, invoke read model interface, return DTO (never entity/aggregate). Application layer does NOT dispatch domain events — that is infrastructure's flush-then-dispatch responsibility.

section.name: deliverables
section.action: List concrete file paths per handler based on USE_CASE_MAP for this BC:
section.template:
For each command use case: - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Command/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}Command.php (DTO) - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Command/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}Handler.php - tests/Unit/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Command/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}HandlerTest.php
For each query use case: - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Query/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}Query.php (DTO) - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Query/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}Handler.php - tests/Unit/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Query/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}HandlerTest.php
If BC has list/search use cases: - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ReadModel/{{ReadModel}}ReadModelInterface.php - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ReadModel/{{ReadModel}}DTO.php - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ReadModel/{{ReadModel}}Criteria.php

section.name: fan-out-hints
section.hint: Single-task goal per BC. Handlers within one BC share the same aggregate ports — splitting would force duplicated mocking setup with no isolation benefit. Multi-task only if BC has >6 handlers.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Unit tests
investigator.pass: Exit 0 AND run is non-empty (test count present in output, NOT "No tests executed!"), all tests green — happy path, validation failure, and domain exception cases covered
investigator.fail: Test failures, errors, or missing coverage for required scenarios

investigator.name: PHPStan
investigator.pass: Exit 0, zero errors — all type hints resolve, interface contracts satisfied
investigator.fail: Type errors, missing return types, or unresolved interface references

investigator.name: Deptrac
investigator.pass: Exit 0 — Application layer depends only on Domain + Share, zero Infrastructure/Controller imports
investigator.fail: Dependency violation: Application importing from Infrastructure or Controller namespace

section.rule: Exactly 3 investigators per application goal (GM-08). Each has type, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Doctrine mappings, database migrations, HTTP controllers, routes, domain logic, event dispatching, infrastructure implementations, Symfony service configuration — all handled by domain or infrastructure goals.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Implement {{BC_NAME}} application layer: command/query handlers and DTOs
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.15.4a (exactly 12 items, PA-01..PA-12)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.15.4b (3 commands)
CONTEXT string from substep 3.15.4c (purpose + deliverables + fan-out hints)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.15.4c's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 3.15.4d
APP_DEPENDS_ON[bc_name] — own BC's domain goal ID from goals.yaml

SCOPE array — see the <scope-derivation> rule in substep 1.7. Derive from this BC's application Deliverables (substep 3.15.4c): emit ["src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/**"] (plus any extra distinct deliverable dirs). Single-BC application namespace ⇒ disjoint from other BCs ⇒ co-schedulable under MaxGoals>1.

substep.rule: Description pattern "Implement {BC} application layer: command/query handlers and DTOs" stays under 120 chars for BC names up to ~40 chars.
max_retries=3 — application goals have focused scope (single BC namespace).

check.action: Print error with full context. Continue to next BC — do not abort the entire loop for a single BC failure.
Print: "Application goal created for {{BC_NAME}}: goal-{{ID}} (phase=application, depends_on=[{{DOMAIN_GOAL_ID}}]) with 3 investigators, {{HANDLER_COUNT}} handlers, max_retries=3"

substep.title: Write goal.md
substep.action: Write a goal.md file in the goal directory with these sections:
substep.template:
## Description Implement {{BC_NAME}} application layer: command/query handlers for {{HANDLER_COUNT}} use cases. Command handlers receive DTOs, orchestrate domain via repository interfaces, return void/scalar. Query handlers receive query DTOs, invoke read model interfaces, return DTOs.
## Dependencies - {{DOMAIN_GOAL_ID}}: {{BC_NAME}} domain layer (provides aggregate interfaces and value objects)
## Deliverables (File paths from substep 3.15.4c deliverables section)
## Acceptance Criteria PA-01 through PA-12 (from substep 3.15.4a)
## Fan-out Hints Single-task goal. All handlers in {{BC_NAME}} share the same aggregate ports — splitting would duplicate mocking setup with no isolation benefit. Multi-task only if handler count > 6.
## Preconditions (Declarative env/service gates the daemon evaluates before dispatching this goal, and re-evaluates on the §5 auto-resume loop. One line each: "- [env] VAR_NAME — remedy" or "- [service] host:port — remedy". Write "none" if the goal has no external precondition.) none
## Not In Scope Doctrine mappings, database migrations, HTTP controllers, routes, domain logic, event dispatching, infrastructure implementations, Symfony service configuration.

substep.title: Report completion
substep.action: After all BCs are processed, print summary:
Proceed to Step 3.16 (Infrastructure).

step.title: Generate infrastructure goals
step.rule: Steps 0-2 must have completed successfully (goal-001 and goal-002 created, plus domain and application goals for all BCs) before this step executes.
All inputs are read from docs/architecture/* files and templates on disk — never from conversation context (G-16).
One infrastructure goal per BC, phase=infrastructure. Each depends_on its own BC's application goal ID.

substep.title: Resolve infrastructure templates
substep.action: Read the infrastructure layer goal template:
Store as INFRA_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used.
Read the context-mapping template for ACL adapter patterns:
Store as CONTEXT_MAP_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used.
Read the fixtures template for integration test setUp pattern:
Store as FIXTURES_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used.

substep.title: Read architecture inputs
substep.action: Read the following files and extract structured data per BC:

read.extract: All level-3 headings under "## Bounded Context Inventory" — the BC names. Reuse from Step 0 if available.
For each BC, collect all aggregate roots, entities, and value objects. Count entities per BC (aggregate roots + child entities) for fan-out heuristic.

For each BC, identify value objects needing custom DBAL types — those wrapping compound data (Money = amount+currency), custom formats (Email with validation), or enums. Simple ID string VOs that map to VARCHAR do not need custom types.
For each BC, find relationships where this BC is the consumer (downstream). Collect the upstream BC name for ACL adapter generation.

substep.rule: For single-BC projects, CROSS_BC_DEPS is empty for every BC — no ACL adapters needed (D-27).

substep.title: Iterate BCs and generate infrastructure goals
substep.action: For each BC in BC_LIST (in dependency order from bounded-contexts.md):

per-bc.action: Look up this BC's application goal ID from previously generated goals. Store as APP_GOAL_ID.
Collect from ENTITY_MAP: aggregate names ({aggregate_list}), entity names ({entity_list}), entity count ({entity_count}).
Collect from VO_TYPE_MAP: VOs needing DBAL types ({vo_type_list}). May be empty.
Collect from CROSS_BC_DEPS: upstream BCs this BC consumes from ({acl_list}). May be empty.
Determine fan-out hint: if entity_count > 3 → multi-task (task-0 for shared infra: services.yaml, base DBAL types; task-1..N per aggregate: mapping + repo + tests). Otherwise → single-task.
Build ACCEPTANCE array parameterized for this BC:

criteria.item: Doctrine XML mapping exists for every aggregate root and entity: {entity_list}
XML mappings live in src/{BC}/Infrastructure/Persistence/Doctrine/Mapping/
No Doctrine annotations/attributes anywhere in src/{BC}/Domain/ or src/{BC}/Application/
Write repository implements {RepoInterface} from Domain layer
Write repository lives in src/{BC}/Infrastructure/Persistence/
Write repository dispatches domain events after flush (flush-then-dispatch pattern)
Read model repository implements Application read model interface
Custom DBAL types created for: {vo_type_list}, registered in Doctrine config
doctrine:schema:validate passes
Migration generated via doctrine:migrations:diff and applies cleanly
Integration tests use real test database, no mock() calls on repositories
Integration tests reset DB state via doctrine:fixtures:load --group=test before each test
Integration tests cover: persist + retrieve aggregate, query methods, edge cases
Integration tests run green: vendor/bin/phpunit --filter={BC}\\Infrastructure passes
services.yaml (or DI extension) binds interfaces to implementations
ACL adapters exist for cross-BC dependencies: {acl_list} in src/{BC}/Infrastructure/ACL/

per-bc.rule: Include PI-08 only when vo_type_list is non-empty. Include PI-16 only when acl_list is non-empty. All other criteria are always present.
Validate command for PI-03 must return 0 (zero annotation leaks). All other commands must exit 0.
per-bc.action: Build CONTEXT string with Investigation Config:
per-bc.section: Infrastructure goal for {BC}: Doctrine XML mappings, repository implementations (write with flush-then-dispatch, read with query interface), migrations, custom DBAL types, ACL adapters, and integration tests with real DB. Aggregates: {aggregate_list}. Entities: {entity_list}. VOs needing DBAL types: {vo_type_list} (or "none"). Cross-BC dependencies (ACL adapters needed): {acl_list} (or "none — single-BC or no inbound deps"). See docs/architecture/test-environment.md for DB config. Fixtures reset via doctrine:fixtures:load --group=test. Infrastructure template: .tmux-cli/templates/_base/infrastructure.md. ACL adapter pattern: .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/context-mapping.md (Messenger query bus).

section.name: fan-out-hints

task.name: Shared infrastructure
task.scope: services.yaml interface bindings, base DBAL types (if any), shared config

task.name: Per-aggregate
task.scope: XML mapping + write repo + read repo + integration tests for one aggregate

task.name: Migration + ACL
task.scope: Generate migration from all mappings, ACL adapters (if any)
task.depends_on: all per-aggregate tasks (needs all mappings before diff)

section.rule: File-disjoint: task-0 owns config/types, task-1..N own per-aggregate files, task-last owns migrations+ACL. Zero overlap.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Schema validation
investigator.pass: Exit 0 — all XML mappings valid, schema in sync
investigator.fail: Mapping errors or schema drift

investigator.name: No annotation leak
investigator.pass: Count is 0 — no Doctrine annotations outside Infrastructure
investigator.fail: Non-zero count — annotations found in Domain or Application layer

investigator.name: Integration tests
investigator.pass: Exit 0 AND run is non-empty (test count present, NOT "No tests executed!"), all tests green
investigator.fail: Test failures or errors

investigator.name: ACL adapter presence
investigator.pass: File count matches number of cross-BC dependencies
investigator.fail: Missing ACL adapter for one or more dependencies

section.rule: Minimum 3 investigators (Schema + Annotations + Tests). 4th (ACL) only when cross-BC deps exist (GM-08).

per-bc.value: HTTP controllers, domain model changes, Application layer changes, E2E tests, Messenger transport config, fixtures (separate goal), hand-written migrations.

params.param: Implement {BC} infrastructure: Doctrine XML mappings, repos, migration, integration tests
ACCEPTANCE array (14-16 items from PI-01..PI-16, conditional on vo_type_list and acl_list)
VALIDATE array (4 commands)
CONTEXT string (purpose + fan-out hints if entity_count > 3)
Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from this goal's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE

SCOPE array — see the <scope-derivation> rule in substep 1.7. Derive from this BC's infrastructure Deliverables: emit ["src/{BC}/Infrastructure/**"] plus any distinct mapping/migration dirs the deliverables touch. Single-BC infrastructure footprint ⇒ disjoint from other BCs ⇒ co-schedulable under MaxGoals>1.

per-bc.rule: Description is parameterized with BC name — must stay under 120 chars (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 — infrastructure goals have focused scope per BC.
depends_on=[APP_GOAL_ID] — each infrastructure goal depends on its own BC's application goal.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "{BC} infrastructure created: goal-{NNN} (phase=infrastructure, depends_on=[{APP_GOAL_ID}]) with {investigator_count} investigators, fan-out={fan_out_hint}, max_retries=3"

substep.title: Verify infrastructure goal count
substep.action: After all BC iterations complete, verify that exactly len(BC_LIST) infrastructure goals were created.

check.action: Print error: "Expected {bc_count} infrastructure goals, created {actual_count}. Missing BCs: {missing_list}"
ABORT.
Print: "Infrastructure generation complete: {bc_count} goals created (goal-{first_id}..goal-{last_id}), all phase=infrastructure"
Proceed to Step 3.17 (Fixtures).

step.title: Generate Auth Bootstrap goal (conditional, phase=auth-bootstrap)
step.rule: This step runs AFTER infrastructure (3.16) and BEFORE fixtures (3.17), controller actions (3.18), and auth flows (3.19) — every downstream auth/protected goal depends on the bootstrap goal, so it must exist first.
Emit the auth-bootstrap goal ONLY when N_auth_flows >= 1. When N_auth_flows == 0, SKIP entirely — downstream deps resolve to empty without error.
Security wiring is OWNED by this single goal — never leave security.yaml / User entity as a side-effect of the first auth flow goal (D3 determinism gate).
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files and discovery output on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Read auth-flow inventory and count flows
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md — the SAME auth-flow source used by step 3.19.1. Build the auth-flow inventory and count N_auth_flows.

check.action: Print: "No auth flows discovered (N_auth_flows == 0) — skipping auth-bootstrap goal. Downstream auth/action deps resolve empty."
SKIP this step entirely. Do NOT create any goal. Proceed to Step 3.17 (Fixtures).

substep.title: Resolve scaffold goal ID
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=scaffold — store its ID as SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID.

check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate auth-bootstrap goal: no scaffold goal (phase=scaffold) found in goals.yaml — ensure Step 2 ran first"

substep.title: Read auth method (session vs JWT)
substep.action: Read the auth method from docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md security section and the discovery `uses_jwt` flag (php-symfony/discovery.md §10a security, php-symfony/libraries.md `{{#uses_jwt}}`).
If uses_jwt is true → JWT identity provider via lexik/jwt-authentication-bundle + keypair generation. If false → Symfony session/form-login authenticator.
substep.rule: The library is resolvable by convention (`lexik/jwt-authentication-bundle` gated on uses_jwt, else `symfony/security-bundle`) — do not escalate unless the JWT choice is genuinely unknowable from project context.
substep.action: Resolve the User entity placement: the identity/auth BC named in discovery. If no dedicated identity BC exists, default to src/Share/ (Share-ownership convention) — do not invent a new BC.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array. AU-08 and AU-09 are OWNED here (relocated from the per-flow auth goals):

criteria.item: Security configuration uses Symfony security bundle properly (firewall, provider, authenticator in config/packages/security.yaml)
User entity has proper password hashing (bcrypt/argon2) and implements the security user interface
JWT keypair generated via bin/console lexik:jwt:generate-keypair and lexik config present at config/packages/lexik_jwt_authentication.yaml

substep.rule: Include AB-JWT only when uses_jwt is true. AU-08/AU-09 are always present.

substep.title: Compose context with deliverables and Investigation Config

section.name: deliverables
section.deliverable: JWT config: config/packages/lexik_jwt_authentication.yaml + keypair via bin/console lexik:jwt:generate-keypair

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Security config presence
investigator.pass: security.yaml and User entity exist; firewall/provider grep returns >= 1
investigator.fail: Missing security.yaml, missing User entity, or no firewall/provider config

investigator.name: Auth wiring check
investigator.pass: Exit 0 — JWT keypair/config valid (uses_jwt) or firewall configured (session)
investigator.fail: JWT config invalid or firewall not configured

investigator.name: Quality gates
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/ --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check src/ && vendor/bin/deptrac analyse
investigator.pass: PHPStan, ECS, and Deptrac all exit 0
investigator.fail: Any tool reports errors or violations

section.rule: 3 investigators (GM-08 minimum is 2). Investigator 2's command is gated on uses_jwt (JWT key/config check vs firewall check).

substep.title: Create the auth-bootstrap goal via goal-create
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE:
substep.value: Auth endpoint controllers/DTOs/routes (built by per-flow auth goals 3.19), business logic beyond authentication, fixtures/seed users (built by fixtures goal), non-auth controller actions.
substep.action: Call goal-create MCP tool:

params.param: Auth bootstrap: security.yaml firewall/provider/authenticator + User entity with password hashing
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.16a.4 (AU-08, AU-09, + AB-JWT when uses_jwt)
CONTEXT string from substep 3.16a.5 (purpose + deliverables)
Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.16a.5's investigators (3; types from the Reference Table).
NOT_IN_SCOPE
[SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID]

substep.rule: phase=auth-bootstrap on the created goal.
depends_on=[SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID] — bootstrap leans on the project skeleton + bundle install only.
Description must be ≤120 chars (MaxGoalDescriptionLength invariant).
substep.action: Store the returned goal ID as AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID — used by substeps 3.18.2 (auth_required actions) and 3.19.2 (auth flows).

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Auth bootstrap created: goal-{NNN} (phase=auth-bootstrap, depends_on=[{SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID}], uses_jwt={uses_jwt}) with 3 investigators, max_retries=3"
Proceed to Step 3.17 (Fixtures).

step.title: Generate Fixtures goal (single goal, phase=fixtures)
step.rule: All per-BC infrastructure goals MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs. If no infrastructure goals are found, ABORT with error.
This is a SINGLE goal — never split per-BC. All fixtures load together because they share FK dependencies.
depends_on MUST include EVERY infrastructure goal ID — dynamically collected from goals.yaml, not hardcoded (G-03).

substep.title: Collect all infrastructure goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter all goal entries where phase=infrastructure. Collect their goal IDs into INFRA_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: ABORT with error: "No infrastructure goals found in goals.yaml — cannot generate fixtures goal without schema dependencies"

substep.action: Print: "Found {{count(INFRA_GOAL_IDS)}} infrastructure goals: {{INFRA_GOAL_IDS}}"
substep.rule: This MUST be dynamic — BC count varies per project. Never hardcode goal IDs.

substep.title: Build entity inventory from domain model
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/domain-model.md. Extract all aggregate/entity names across all BCs from AGGREGATE_MAP (built in Step 0).
For each entity, build fixture class path: src/{BC}/Infrastructure/DataFixtures/{Entity}Fixtures.php
Additionally include UserFixtures (requires UserPasswordHasherInterface injection) — one per BC that has auth-related entities, or a shared UserFixtures if auth is centralized.
Read docs/architecture/api-endpoints.md to identify which entities have list/search endpoints — these need page_size+1 records for pagination testing (FX-04).

substep.title: Resolve fixtures template
substep.action: Attempt to read the language-specific overlay template first, then fall back to base:
Store the resolved template content as FIXTURES_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used (overlay or base).
substep.rule: Fixture class patterns (DependentFixtureInterface, idempotency strategy, CLI commands) MUST come from the resolved template, not hardcoded in this XML.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (FX-01..FX-06)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 6 fixture criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:698-706:

criteria.item: Fixture class exists for every aggregate/entity that E2E tests reference
Fixtures create test users matching docs/architecture/test-environment.md credentials — never hardcoded
Fixtures are idempotent — running doctrine:fixtures:load twice doesn't fail
Fixtures create page_size+1 records per entity for list/pagination tests
bin/console doctrine:fixtures:load --env=test runs green (exit code 0)
Fixtures depend on schema being created — migrations applied first via depends_on

substep.rule: All 6 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with commands that verify fixtures work correctly:

commands.cmd: Verify fixture class count matches entity count from domain-model.md

substep.rule: Validation must prove idempotency by running the load command twice sequentially — both must exit 0.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config and fan-out hint
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Fixtures goal creates test data for all entities across all bounded contexts. Single goal because fixtures share FK dependencies and must load in correct order via DependentFixtureInterface. All entity names from docs/architecture/domain-model.md. Test user credentials from docs/architecture/test-environment.md (never hardcoded). Fixture class patterns from {{FIXTURES_TEMPLATE}} (DependentFixtureInterface, #[AsFixture(group: 'test')], idempotent purge, page_size+1 records). Depends on ALL infrastructure goals: {{INFRA_GOAL_IDS}} — these provide Doctrine mappings, migrations, and custom DBAL types.

section.name: fan-out-hint
section.hint: single-task — all fixtures must load together since they share FK dependencies. Doctrine's DependentFixtureInterface handles internal ordering.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Fixture load
investigator.pass: Exit code 0 — all fixtures loaded successfully
investigator.fail: Non-zero exit, missing table, FK constraint violation, or missing service

investigator.name: Idempotency check
investigator.pass: Both runs exit code 0 — purge-and-reload produces identical state
investigator.fail: Second run fails (constraint violation, duplicate key, or stale reference)

investigator.name: Fixture class coverage
investigator.commands: For every entity in docs/architecture/domain-model.md, verify src/{BC}/Infrastructure/DataFixtures/{Entity}Fixtures.php exists
investigator.pass: All fixture files present — one per testable entity
investigator.fail: Missing fixture class for one or more entities

section.rule: Exactly 3 investigators (GM-08 allows 2-4). Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Production seed data, performance benchmarks, event listener test data, fixture performance optimization — production seeding is a separate concern; event listener test data added by event listener goals if needed.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Create test fixtures for all entities with test users and pagination data
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.17.4 (exactly 6 items, FX-01..FX-06)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.17.5
CONTEXT string from substep 3.17.6 (purpose + fan-out hint)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.17.6's 3 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 3.17.7
INFRA_GOAL_IDS — ALL infrastructure goal IDs collected in substep 3.17.1

substep.rule: Description is 73 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 (G-11) — fixture issues are typically FK ordering or missing entity, fixable in few cycles.
depends_on MUST be the FULL INFRA_GOAL_IDS list — never just the last one (G-03).

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Fixtures created: goal-P+1 (phase=fixtures, depends_on={{INFRA_GOAL_IDS}}) with 3 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3"
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Controller Path Resolver (shared)
step.rule: This step defines the SINGLE source of truth for every controller file path the planner produces. Both the action step (3.18) and the auth step (3.19) MUST route their controller deliverable, investigator file list, and phpstan/ecs paths through RESOLVE_CONTROLLER_PATH. No other location may restate the canonical controller-path shape — they reference this resolver by name.

resolver.name: RESOLVE_CONTROLLER_PATH

inputs.input: Controller base name — the action name or auth-flow name (e.g. CreateBooking, Login).
