normalization.rule: Strip a trailing "Controller" from NAME before appending the "Controller.php" suffix. So NAME=Login yields LoginController.php, and a NAME that already arrives as LoginController still yields LoginController.php — never LoginControllerController.php.

If a candidate controller path lacks the "/Controller/" segment immediately after "Infrastructure/Http/" (e.g. a Fan-Out value or a legacy string of the form src/{BC}/Infrastructure/Http/{NAME}Controller.php), insert "/Controller/" after "Infrastructure/Http/" so it matches the canonical shape above.

resolver.result: The canonical controller path. The produces line above is the ONLY place the full canonical shape is stated; every controller reference elsewhere uses RESOLVE_CONTROLLER_PATH(BC, NAME).

step.title: Generate Controller Action Goals
step.rule: Step 3.17 (Fixtures) must have completed successfully before this step executes — action E2E tests need fixture data.
One goal per non-auth controller action (G-05). Auth endpoints are handled by a separate auth step (3.19) — never count them here.
All inputs are read from docs/architecture/* files and templates on disk — never from conversation context (G-16).

substep.title: Parse api-endpoints.md and filter auth
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/api-endpoints.md. Parse the file into two regions:

parse.region: All ### blocks under this heading are non-auth actions. Extract each ### {action_name} block with: action_name, http_method, endpoint_path, bc_name (from "Bounded Context" table cell), description, auth_required, request fields, success_status_code, error responses, and Fan-Out paths (Handler, Request DTO, Response DTO, Route, E2E Test).
SKIP this entire section and all its sub-blocks. These are counted as N_auth_flows and handled by the auth goal step (3.19).

substep.action: As a secondary guard, also exclude any action whose name matches auth patterns (register, login, logout, password-reset, token-refresh) even if misplaced outside Auth Flows section.
Store the filtered list as ACTION_LIST. Record ACTION_COUNT = len(ACTION_LIST).
substep.rule: ACTION_COUNT must equal N_actions in the goal count formula (spec line 276) — non-auth actions only.

substep.title: Resolve depends_on for action goals
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. For each action in ACTION_LIST, resolve depends_on from previously generated goal IDs:

dependencies.dep: Filter goals.yaml for phase=infrastructure where BC name matches this action's bc_name. Required — every action depends on its BC's infra layer being built.
Filter goals.yaml for phase=fixtures. Required — E2E tests need test data.
Filter goals.yaml for phase matching error-handling or cross-cutting with error-handling description. Required — E2E tests assert RFC 7807 error responses.
Filter goals.yaml for phase=auth. Required — protected endpoint E2E tests need auth infrastructure for 401/403 assertions.

Filter goals.yaml for phase=auth-bootstrap. For every action whose auth_required flag is true, add AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID — the protected endpoint needs the security firewall/provider + User entity from the bootstrap goal. Skip for public (auth_required=false) actions.

substep.action: Collect these goal IDs into DEPENDS_ON array for each action: [own_bc_infra_goal_id, fixtures_goal_id, error_handling_goal_id] + all_auth_goal_ids + (AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID when this action is auth_required).
substep.rule: If fixtures or error handling goal ID cannot be resolved, ABORT — prior steps did not run correctly.
Auth goal IDs may be empty if the project has no auth flows — that is valid. Infrastructure goal for the action's BC must always resolve.

AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID is appended ONLY for auth_required actions. If no auth flows exist (3.16a skipped), there is no bootstrap goal and no action is auth_required — the append is empty, which is valid.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (PC-01..PC-17)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 17 controller action criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:630-646:

criteria.item: Controller class has maximum ~20 lines in action method
Controller action: deserialize → dispatch → serialize (nothing else)
Request DTO has Symfony validation constraints matching business rules
Response DTO serializes data, does not expose internal domain structure
Route is configured (YAML or attributes) with correct method + path
Controller imports only from Application layer (handlers, DTOs), never Domain directly
Playwright E2E test exists at tests/E2E/{BC}/{ActionName}Test.{ext}
Playwright test resets fixtures before running
Playwright test uses credentials from docs/architecture/test-environment.md
Playwright test verifies: correct status code
Playwright test verifies: response body structure and values
Playwright test verifies: error cases (422 for invalid input, 401 for no auth)
Playwright test verifies: state change in DB (if applicable)
Playwright test passes when run individually
PHPStan level 9 passes on controller file
ECS passes on all new files
Deptrac passes — controller imports Application layer only

branch.action: Reduce ACCEPTANCE array to 9 criteria: PC-01..PC-06, PC-15..PC-17. Exclude PC-07..PC-14 (all Playwright E2E criteria).
Print warning: "HAS_FRONTEND=false — Playwright E2E criteria PC-07..PC-14 excluded from controller action goals. 9 criteria remain."
branch.rule: E2E test deliverables (tests/E2E/{BC}/{ActionName}Test.{ext}) are also excluded from the deliverables list for this action goal.
All 17 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.rule: PC-07..PC-14 are conditional on Playwright availability. If Playwright status in test-environment.md is "not installed" or "not applicable", reduce to PC-01..PC-06, PC-15..PC-17 (9 criteria) and note E2E tests are out of scope for this goal.

substep.title: Compose validation commands per action
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array per action using the Fan-Out paths from api-endpoints.md:
substep.rule: Request/Response DTO, route, and e2e paths come from the Fan-Out section of each action block in api-endpoints.md — use them directly. The controller path is produced by RESOLVE_CONTROLLER_PATH (3.17.0), the single source of truth — do not derive any other path from conventions.
PC-14 command is conditional on Playwright availability from test-environment.md.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config per action
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string per action with these sections:
substep.section: This controller action implements the HTTP adapter for {METHOD} {endpoint_path} in the {bc_name} bounded context. The controller is a thin adapter: deserialize request → dispatch command/query to Application layer → serialize response. See docs/architecture/api-endpoints.md for request/response schema, validation rules, and error responses. See docs/architecture/test-environment.md for E2E test credentials and base URL — never duplicate credentials (GM-05). See docs/architecture/test-strategy.md for E2E test layer conventions.

section.name: deliverables
section.action: Bind controller_path = RESOLVE_CONTROLLER_PATH({bc_name}, {action_name}) (3.17.0) ONCE for this action. Then list the 5 deliverables (GM-12) using Fan-Out paths from api-endpoints.md for DTO/route/e2e and controller_path for the controller:

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: File existence
investigator.pass: All 5 deliverable files exist on disk
investigator.fail: One or more deliverable files missing

investigator.name: Quality gates
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse {controller_path} --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check {controller_path} {request_dto_path} {response_dto_path} && vendor/bin/deptrac analyse
investigator.pass: PHPStan, ECS, and Deptrac all exit 0 — controller imports Application layer only, code style clean
investigator.fail: Any quality tool reports errors or Deptrac violations

investigator.name: E2E test
investigator.pass: E2E test passes — correct status code, response body, error cases verified
investigator.fail: Test failure, timeout, or missing test file

section.rule: Investigator 3 is conditional on Playwright availability. If not available, include only investigators 1 and 2 (minimum 2 per GM-08).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope per action
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Domain logic (aggregate roots, value objects, domain events, repository interfaces — built by domain goals). Application handlers and command/query buses (built by application goals). Infrastructure persistence (Doctrine mappings, migrations — built by infrastructure goals). Auth endpoints (handled by auth goals). Fixtures and seed data (built by fixtures goal). Error handling middleware (built by error handling goal).

substep.title: Iterate and call goal-create for each action
substep.action: For each action in ACTION_LIST, call goal-create MCP with:

params.param: {METHOD} {endpoint_path} — {bc_name} controller action
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.18.3 (17 items if Playwright available, 9 if not)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.18.4 (5 commands if Playwright, 4 if not)
CONTEXT string from substep 3.18.5 (purpose + deliverables)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.18.5's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 3.18.6
DEPENDS_ON from substep 3.18.2 (own BC infra + fixtures + error handling + auth goal IDs)

substep.rule: Description format "{METHOD} {path} — {BC} controller action" ensures uniqueness (G-12) because paths are unique per endpoint.

Description must be ≤120 characters (MaxGoalDescriptionLength invariant). If the natural description exceeds 120 chars, truncate the path or BC name with ellipsis while preserving METHOD and key path segments.
max_retries=3 per G-11.
phase=action on every created goal.

check.condition: goal-create returns error for any action
check.action: Print error with action name, endpoint path, and full context. ABORT — partial goal creation leaves an inconsistent state.
Print: "Controller action goals created: {{ACTION_COUNT}} goals (phase=action), each with {{investigator_count}} investigators, 5 deliverables, max_retries=3"
Verify: created goal count == ACTION_COUNT. If mismatch, print warning with expected vs actual counts.
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate Auth flow goals (phase=auth)
step.rule: Fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) and error handling goal (phase=error-handling) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs. If either is missing, ABORT with error.
Auth flows are counted in N_auth_flows ONLY — never in per-BC N_actions (no double counting).
One goal per auth flow — never batch multiple flows into one goal.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Read auth flow inventory from discovery output
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md. Extract the auth flows section written by discover.xml Step 6.2.
Build AUTH_FLOWS list — each entry has: flow_name (Register, Login, Logout, Token Refresh, Password Reset), description, steps involved.
Count N_auth_flows = length of AUTH_FLOWS.
substep.rule: Only include flows confirmed during discovery. If Password Reset was marked "not in scope", exclude it (AU-04 is conditional).
Token Refresh is a valid auth flow if discovered — do not skip it.

substep.title: Resolve dependency goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=fixtures — store its ID as FIXTURES_GOAL_ID.
Find the goal with phase=error-handling — store its ID as ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID.
Find the goal with phase=auth-bootstrap — store its ID as AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID (created by step 3.16a; always present when N_auth_flows >= 1, which is true here since auth flows exist).
Set AUTH_DEPENDS_ON = [FIXTURES_GOAL_ID, ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID, AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID].

check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate auth goals: no auth-bootstrap goal (phase=auth-bootstrap) found in goals.yaml — step 3.16a must run before 3.19 when auth flows exist"
ABORT with error: "Cannot generate auth goals: no fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) found in goals.yaml — ensure fixtures step ran first"
ABORT with error: "Cannot generate auth goals: no error handling goal (phase=error-handling) found in goals.yaml — ensure error handling step ran first"

substep.title: Map acceptance criteria per auth flow
substep.action: For each auth flow, build ACCEPTANCE array combining applicable AU-01..AU-10 and PC-01..PC-17 criteria:

criteria-map.flow: Registration creates user, hashes password, returns token/session. Depends on auth-bootstrap for security config + User entity (AU-08/AU-09 owned there).
Login authenticates credentials, returns token/session. Tests admin vs user role access. Validates against bootstrap-provided security config.
Logout invalidates session/token. Verifies subsequent request returns 401.
Token refresh renews session without re-authentication.
Password reset flow end-to-end (conditional — only if in scope).

substep.rule: AU-08 (Symfony security bundle config) and AU-09 (password hashing) are OWNED by the auth-bootstrap goal (step 3.16a), NOT by per-flow auth goals. Every per-flow auth goal depends on AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID and keeps only AU-07/AU-10 (plus its flow-specific AU-01..AU-06) — it does not re-declare security.yaml or the User entity.
AU-05, AU-06 (role-based access) are tested within Login and Register goals — admin login returns admin token, user login returns user token. No separate role-access goal needed.
All flows get AU-07 (unauthenticated request returns 401) and AU-10 (test users from test-environment.md).
All flows get the full PC-01..PC-17 controller action criteria — auth endpoints ARE controller actions.

criteria-reference.name: AU-01..AU-10
criteria-reference.item: Registration action creates user, hashes password, returns token/session
Login action authenticates, returns token/session
Logout action invalidates session/token
Password reset flow works end-to-end (if in scope)
Role-based access: admin can access admin endpoints
Role-based access: regular user gets 403 on admin endpoints
Unauthenticated request to protected endpoint returns 401
Security configuration uses Symfony security bundle properly (firewall config)
User entity has proper password hashing (bcrypt/argon2)
Auth tests use dedicated test users from docs/architecture/test-environment.md

criteria-reference.name: PC-01..PC-17
criteria-reference.item: Controller class has maximum ~20 lines in action method
Controller action: deserialize, dispatch, serialize (nothing else)
Request DTO has Symfony validation constraints matching business rules
Response DTO serializes data, does not expose internal domain structure
Route is configured (YAML or attributes) with correct method + path
Controller imports only from Application layer, never Domain directly
Playwright E2E test exists at tests/E2E/{BC}/{ActionName}Test.{ext}
Playwright test resets fixtures before running
Playwright test uses credentials from docs/architecture/test-environment.md
Playwright test verifies: correct status code
Playwright test verifies: response body structure and values
Playwright test verifies: error cases (422 for invalid input, 401 for no auth)
Playwright test verifies: state change in DB (if applicable)
Playwright test passes when run individually
PHPStan level 9 passes on controller file
ECS passes on all new files
Deptrac passes — controller imports Application layer only

substep.title: Compose context with deliverables and Investigation Config
substep.action: For each auth goal, build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Auth endpoint implementing {{flow_name}} flow. Uses Symfony security bundle for authentication/authorization. All auth flows use dedicated test users from docs/architecture/test-environment.md — never hard-code credentials. Security configuration in config/packages/security.yaml — firewall, authenticator, and provider config. See docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md for auth method decisions and flow details.

section.name: deliverables
section.deliverable: Route config: config/routes/{bc}.yaml or PHP attributes
section.note: Security config (config/packages/security.yaml) + User entity are NOT deliverables here — they are provided by the auth-bootstrap dependency (AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID, step 3.16a). This goal consumes them, it does not create them.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Auth endpoint
investigator.pass: Route registered and controller wired (security config + User entity are pre-existing deps from auth-bootstrap, not created here)
investigator.fail: Missing route or controller not found

investigator.name: Quality gates
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check src/ && vendor/bin/deptrac analyse
investigator.pass: All three commands exit 0 — zero errors, zero violations
investigator.fail: Any tool reports errors or violations

investigator.name: E2E test
investigator.pass: Test passes — correct status codes, token/session handling, error responses verified
investigator.fail: Test failure or missing test file

section.rule: 3 investigators per auth goal (GM-08 minimum is 2). Each has type, files, commands, pass/fail (GM-09).

substep.title: Generate one goal per auth flow via goal-create
substep.action: For each AUTH_FLOW in AUTH_FLOWS, call goal-create MCP tool:

params.param: Short title, <=120 chars. Pattern: "Auth: implement {flow_name} endpoint with {key_behavior}" Examples: - "Auth: implement user registration with password hashing and session token" - "Auth: implement login endpoint with credential validation and JWT response" - "Auth: implement logout endpoint with session/token invalidation" - "Auth: implement token refresh endpoint with session renewal" - "Auth: implement password reset flow with email verification"
Combined AU + PC criteria from substep 3.19.3 for this specific flow. Each criterion is a separate string in the array.
[ "npx playwright test tests/E2E/{BC}/{AuthAction}Test.{ext}", "vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/ --level=9", "vendor/bin/ecs check src/", "vendor/bin/deptrac analyse" ]
CONTEXT string from substep 3.19.4 (purpose + deliverables)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.19.4's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
Other auth flows not covered by this goal, business logic beyond authentication/authorization, frontend UI, non-auth controller actions, database migrations.
AUTH_DEPENDS_ON = [FIXTURES_GOAL_ID, ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID] from substep 3.19.2

substep.action: After each successful goal-create, record the returned goal ID. Append to AUTH_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT — do not continue to next flow.

substep.rule: Every auth goal references the existing security config + User entity from the auth-bootstrap dependency (AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID, step 3.16a) — no per-flow goal creates security.yaml or the User entity (D3 determinism gate).
Never hard-code test credentials in goal descriptions or context — always reference docs/architecture/test-environment.md (GM-05, AU-10).
description field max 120 chars (MaxGoalDescriptionLength). Detailed criteria go in acceptance and validate arrays.

substep.title: Record auth goal IDs for downstream dependencies
substep.action: Store AUTH_GOAL_IDS list as a session variable for use by subsequent steps (controller action goals).
Print summary: "Auth goals created: {{N_auth_flows}} goals (phase=auth, depends_on=[{{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}}, {{ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID}}]), IDs: [{{AUTH_GOAL_IDS}}]"
substep.rule: The real auth-infrastructure prerequisite for protected endpoints is AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID (security firewall/provider + User entity), NOT "all auth goals". Subsequent auth_required controller action goals (3.18.2) MUST depend on AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID; the legacy "all AUTH_GOAL_IDS" dependency is retained for 401/403 flow E2E assertions but is no longer the source of the security infra itself.
substep.action: Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate dev-env seed-admin goal (conditional, phase=seed)
step.rule: Runs strictly AFTER step 3.19 so every phase=auth and phase=auth-bootstrap goal ID already exists in goals.yaml.
CONDITIONAL — emit at most ONE goal, and only when seeding was requested AND at least one auth flow exists. NEVER fold this into the test-only fixtures goal (phase=fixtures, --env=test).

Credentials are referenced by env-var name only (admin_identifier_env, admin_password_env) — NEVER inline a secret value in description, context, acceptance, or validate (GM-05). Seeds the dev environment ONLY — NEVER prod, NEVER --env=test.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Read seed decision and gate
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md. Locate the `## Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin` block. Extract seed_default_admin and the env-var NAMES admin_identifier_env, admin_password_env.
Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md auth flows (the SAME source used by step 3.19.1). Count N_auth_flows.

branch.condition: seed_default_admin != yes OR the Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin block is absent
branch.action: Print: "Seed default admin not requested — skipping Step 3.19a."
SKIP — proceed to next step. Do NOT call goal-create.
Print WARNING: "Seed default admin was requested but no auth flows exist — there is no User entity to seed. Skipping Step 3.19a."
SKIP — proceed to next step. Do NOT call goal-create.
Proceed to substep 3.19a.2.

substep.title: Resolve depends_on
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=auth-bootstrap — store its ID as SEED_DEP. The auth-bootstrap goal (step 3.16a) owns the User entity + config/packages/security.yaml, so the seed must run after it.
FALLBACK: if no phase=auth-bootstrap goal exists (D3 not present in this plan), resolve the first phase=auth goal (Register — owner of the User entity per step 3.19) and use its ID as SEED_DEP.

check.condition: neither a phase=auth-bootstrap goal nor any phase=auth goal resolves
check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate seed-admin goal: no auth-bootstrap or auth goal found in goals.yaml — the seed depends on the User entity owner."

substep.action: Set SEED_DEPENDS_ON = [SEED_DEP].

substep.title: Create the dev-env seed-admin goal via goal-create
substep.action: Resolve the seed command name: default to `app:seed-admin` (Symfony `app:` namespace). If a php-symfony overlay customizes the seed command, read it from the resolved template rather than hardcoding (mirror the fixtures-template resolution pattern at 3.17.3).
Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Seed default admin in dev via bin/console app:seed-admin

[ "SA-01: app:seed-admin console command class exists (Symfony app: namespace command)", "SA-02: command reads the admin identifier and password from the named env-vars only (admin_identifier_env / admin_password_env from docs/architecture/test-environment.md) — referenced by env-var name only, never literal values", "SA-03: no literal secret value appears anywhere in the source — credentials are referenced by env-var name only (GM-05)", "SA-04: command is idempotent — a second run creates no duplicate admin and exits without error", "SA-05: bin/console app:seed-admin --env=dev exits 0 (NEVER --env=test)", "SA-06: an admin row is present in the dev database after the command runs" ]

[ "bin/console app:seed-admin --env=dev", "bin/console app:seed-admin --env=dev (second run — idempotent, exit 0, no duplicate)", "assert the seeded admin row exists in the dev database after the run (e.g. bin/console dbal:run-sql to count admin users --env=dev; adapt table/column to the resolved User entity) — NEVER --env=test" ]

Seed a single default admin user into the DEV database so the running app is usable immediately after generation (the fixtures goal only seeds the --env=test database). This goal runs `bin/console app:seed-admin --env=dev` — NEVER --env=test, NEVER prod, and is NEVER folded into the fixtures goal. Admin credentials are read from the environment by NAME only: identifier from {{admin_identifier_env}}, password from {{admin_password_env}} (see docs/architecture/test-environment.md `## Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin`). Never hard-code credentials in source — referenced by env-var name only (GM-05). The User entity and config/packages/security.yaml are provided by the auth-bootstrap dependency (SEED_DEP) — this goal consumes them, it does not create them.

Production seeding; role/permission seeding beyond the single default admin; test fixtures (--env=test, the fixtures goal — this is NEVER folded into the fixtures goal); creating the User entity or config/packages/security.yaml (owned by the auth-bootstrap dependency).
SEED_DEPENDS_ON = [SEED_DEP] from substep 3.19a.2
seed

substep.rule: phase=seed on the created goal (a new phase value, distinct from fixtures).
Description is 56 chars — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength). Detailed criteria live in acceptance and validate.
2 investigators (GM-08 minimum): one command-run (runs --env=dev + asserts the admin row), one file-check (asserts no inlined secret). NEVER add a --env=test invocation.
max_retries=2.

check.action: Print the error with full context and ABORT — do not leave a partial seed goal.
Print: "Seed-admin goal created: goal-{NNN} (phase=seed, depends_on=[{SEED_DEP}], max_retries=2) — runs bin/console app:seed-admin --env=dev, credentials by env-var name only."
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate event listener goals (phase=event_listener)
step.rule: Infrastructure goals (phase=infrastructure) and fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs. If either is missing, ABORT with error.
One goal per event listener — never batch multiple listeners into one goal.

Integration tests only — no Playwright E2E tests for event handlers (spec lines 329-332). Action E2E tests assert HTTP response + direct DB state; event side effects are tested here via integration tests.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Read event inventory from discovery output
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/domain-model.md. For each BC, collect EVENTS_PER_AGGREGATE — the domain events listed under each aggregate's events section (written by discover.xml Step 3).

Read docs/architecture/context-mapping.md (if it exists — skipped for single-BC projects per D-27). Identify cross-BC event flows: producer BC, consumer BC, Published Language event DTO, ACL adapter needed.
Build EVENT_LISTENER_LIST where each entry contains:

fields.field: event_name — the domain event class name (past tense, e.g. ReservationConfirmed)
source_bc — the BC that raises the event (aggregate root's BC)
listener_bc — the BC that handles the event (same as source_bc for intra-BC, different for cross-BC)
acl_needed — true if is_cross_bc (ACL adapter translates Published Language event to local domain language)
side_effect_summary — short description of what the handler does (from discovery output)

substep.action: Count N_event_listeners = length of EVENT_LISTENER_LIST.

branch.action: Print: "No event listeners found in discovery output — skipping Step 3.20"
SKIP — proceed to next step. Do NOT call goal-create.
Print: "Event listeners found: {{N_event_listeners}}. Building goals."
Proceed to substep 3.20.2.

substep.title: Resolve dependency goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk.
For each event listener, find:

fields.field: OWN_BC_INFRA_GOAL_ID — the infrastructure goal (phase=infrastructure) for the listener's BC (listener_bc)

substep.action: For cross-BC listeners (is_cross_bc == true), also find:

fields.field: SOURCE_BC_INFRA_GOAL_ID — the infrastructure goal for the source BC (event dispatch must exist before listener can consume)

substep.action: Set per-listener DEPENDS_ON:
substep.rule: Intra-BC listener: DEPENDS_ON = [OWN_BC_INFRA_GOAL_ID, FIXTURES_GOAL_ID]
Cross-BC listener: DEPENDS_ON = [OWN_BC_INFRA_GOAL_ID, SOURCE_BC_INFRA_GOAL_ID, FIXTURES_GOAL_ID]

check.condition: OWN_BC_INFRA_GOAL_ID not found for any listener
check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate event listener goal: no infrastructure goal (phase=infrastructure) found for BC {{listener_bc}} in goals.yaml"
ABORT with error: "Cannot generate event listener goals: no fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) found in goals.yaml — ensure fixtures step ran first"

substep.title: Map acceptance criteria (EV-01..EV-13)
substep.action: For each event listener, build ACCEPTANCE array from EV-01..EV-13, with conditional criteria based on is_cross_bc and async transport:

criteria-reference.name: EV-01..EV-13
criteria-reference.item: Event class exists in Domain layer with correct past-tense name — file at src/{source_bc}/Domain/Event/{EventName}.php
Event is recorded by aggregate root method (collected via DomainEventTrait, not dispatched inline)
Event is dispatched by infrastructure repository after flush (flush-then-dispatch pattern)
Domain event dispatcher interface exists in Domain layer
Symfony Messenger adapter implements domain event dispatcher interface in Infrastructure layer
Listener/handler class exists in appropriate layer — file at {handler_class_path}
Listener is registered in Symfony DI (auto-configured via #[AsMessageHandler] or explicit services.yaml entry) — verify via bin/console debug:messenger
Published Language event DTO used in src/Share/Event/{EventName}.php — not internal domain event class
ACL adapter translates incoming Published Language event to local domain language — adapter at src/{listener_bc}/Infrastructure/ACL/
Integration test proves: trigger action that raises event, event dispatched, handler executes, side effect verified
Integration test uses real database, not mocks on repositories — no mock() calls on repository interfaces
Async listener has proper Messenger transport config in messenger.yaml with doctrine://default or configured DSN
Integration tests run green — vendor/bin/phpunit --filter=EventListener passes

substep.rule: EV-01..EV-07 always included. EV-08/EV-09 only when is_cross_bc. EV-10/EV-11/EV-13 always included. EV-12 only when async transport is selected (check docs/architecture/adrs/ or cross-cutting.md for Messenger async decision).
Replace placeholders ({source_bc}, {listener_bc}, {EventName}, {handler_class_path}) with actual values from EVENT_LISTENER_LIST.

substep.title: Compose context with deliverables and Investigation Config
substep.action: For each event listener goal, build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Event listener: {{event_name}} handler in {{listener_bc}}. Listens for {{event_name}} raised by {{source_bc}} aggregate and executes: {{side_effect_summary}}. See docs/architecture/domain-model.md for event definition and docs/architecture/context-mapping.md for cross-BC flow. Integration tests only — no Playwright E2E (event side effects tested via PHPUnit integration tests).

section.name: deliverables
section.deliverable: DI configuration: services.yaml entry or #[AsMessageHandler] attribute
ACL adapter: src/{listener_bc}/Infrastructure/ACL/{source_bc}ACLAdapter.php (if not already created by infra goal)
Published Language DTO: src/Share/Event/{EventName}.php (if not already created by domain goal)
Messenger transport config: config/packages/messenger.yaml routing entry for this event

section.name: investigation-config
section.action: Build 2-3 investigators per event listener goal:

investigator.name: Event wiring
investigator.pass: Event collected in aggregate via DomainEventTrait, dispatched by repo after flush, listener registered in DI, handler receives correct event class
investigator.fail: Missing event class, listener not registered, event not dispatched by repo, or handler receives wrong event type
investigator.condition_extra: Also verify: ACL adapter exists in src/{listener_bc}/Infrastructure/ACL/, Published Language DTO in src/Share/Event/, handler imports only from own BC + Share namespaces

investigator.name: Integration tests
investigator.pass: Exit 0, all event listener integration tests green — event fires, handler executes, side effect verified in real DB
investigator.fail: Test failure, missing test coverage, or mock() on repository interfaces detected

investigator.name: Quality gates
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/{listener_bc}/Infrastructure/ --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check src/{listener_bc}/Infrastructure/
investigator.pass: PHPStan and ECS both exit 0 — zero errors on event listener and adapter files
investigator.fail: Type errors or code style violations in listener/adapter files

section.rule: 2-3 investigators per event listener goal (GM-08 allows 2-4). Cross-BC listeners get 3 (extra ACL checks in investigator 1). Intra-BC listeners get 2-3. Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Generate one goal per event listener via goal-create
substep.action: For each EVENT_LISTENER in EVENT_LISTENER_LIST, call goal-create MCP tool:

params.param: Short title, <=120 chars. Pattern: "Event listener: {EventName} handler in {listener_bc} — {side_effect_summary}" Examples: - "Event listener: ReservationConfirmed handler in Notification — send confirmation email" - "Event listener: PaymentCompleted handler in Booking — update reservation payment status" - "Event listener: GuestCheckedIn handler in Housekeeping — schedule room cleaning"
EV-01..EV-13 criteria from substep 3.20.3 for this specific listener. Each criterion is a separate string in the array. Conditional criteria (EV-08, EV-09, EV-12) included only when applicable.

[ "vendor/bin/phpunit --filter=EventListener", "vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/{listener_bc}/Infrastructure/ --level=9", "vendor/bin/ecs check src/{listener_bc}/Infrastructure/", "bin/console debug:messenger" ]
CONTEXT string from substep 3.20.4 (purpose + deliverables)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.20.4's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.

Domain event class definition (handled by domain goal), aggregate root changes (handled by domain goal), infrastructure repository dispatch setup (handled by infra goal), Playwright E2E tests (event side effects tested via integration tests only), other event listeners not covered by this goal.
DEPENDS_ON from substep 3.20.2 (own BC infra + fixtures, optionally source BC infra for cross-BC)

substep.action: After each successful goal-create, record the returned goal ID. Append to EVENT_LISTENER_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: Print error with event name, listener BC, and full context. ABORT — do not continue to next listener.

substep.rule: Description must be ≤120 characters (MaxGoalDescriptionLength invariant). If the natural description exceeds 120 chars, truncate the side_effect_summary while preserving EventName and BC.
max_retries=3 per G-11.
phase=event_listener on every created goal.
No Playwright E2E commands in validate array — integration tests only (vendor/bin/phpunit).

substep.title: Record event listener goal IDs and report
substep.action: Store EVENT_LISTENER_GOAL_IDS list as a session variable for downstream reference.
Print summary: "Event listener goals created: {{N_event_listeners}} goals (phase=event_listener), IDs: [{{EVENT_LISTENER_GOAL_IDS}}]"
For each goal, print: " - goal-{{ID}}: {{event_name}} in {{listener_bc}} (depends_on={{DEPENDS_ON}}, cross_bc={{is_cross_bc}})"
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate Error Handling goal (single goal, phase=error_handling)
step.rule: Fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs. If not found, ABORT with error.
This is a SINGLE goal — error handling is cross-cutting (exception listener, RFC 7807 response factory, status code mapping) and tightly coupled. Never split.
This step is UNCONDITIONAL — every DDD project needs global error handling. No skip logic.
depends_on MUST include the fixtures goal ID — error handling integration tests need seeded data to trigger exception scenarios.

substep.title: Collect fixtures goal ID
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter for phase=fixtures. Store the goal ID as FIXTURES_GOAL_ID.

check.action: ABORT with error: "No fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) found in goals.yaml — cannot generate error handling goal without fixture dependencies"

substep.action: Print: "Found fixtures goal: {{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}}"
substep.rule: FIXTURES_GOAL_ID MUST be dynamically resolved from goals.yaml — never hardcoded (G-03).

substep.title: Resolve error handling template
substep.action: Attempt to read the language-specific overlay template first, then fall back to base:
Store the resolved template content as ERROR_HANDLING_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used (overlay or base).
substep.rule: Exception listener patterns, RFC 7807 format structure, and status code mapping conventions MUST come from the resolved template, not hardcoded in this XML.

substep.title: Read architecture inputs
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/domain-model.md. Extract domain exception types across all BCs — DomainException, EntityNotFoundException, AccessDeniedException, ValidationException, and any BC-specific exceptions.
Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md. Find error handling decisions: RFC 7807 Problem Details format, status code mapping rules, whether internal details are exposed in production.
Store extracted exception types as DOMAIN_EXCEPTIONS and error handling decisions as EH_DECISIONS.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (EH-01..EH-08)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 8 error handling criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:712-723:

criteria-reference.name: EH-01..EH-08
criteria-reference.item: Symfony exception listener registered and catches all unhandled exceptions — verify via bin/console debug:event-dispatcher
All error responses use RFC 7807 Problem Details format — verify type/title/status/detail fields in response
DomainException maps to 422 — integration test
EntityNotFoundException maps to 404 — integration test
AccessDeniedException maps to 403 — integration test
ValidationException maps to 422 with field-level errors — integration test
Unexpected exceptions map to 500 with no internal details exposed — integration test
PHPStan clean, ECS clean on error handling files — static analysis passes

substep.rule: All 8 criteria are UNCONDITIONAL — every project gets all of them. No condition= attributes.
All 8 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with commands that verify error handling works correctly:
substep.rule: 4 validation commands covering listener registration (EH-01), integration tests (EH-03..EH-07), and static analysis (EH-08).

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Error handling goal: implement global exception listener, RFC 7807 Problem Details response factory, and domain exception → HTTP status code mapping. Exception types from docs/architecture/domain-model.md: {{DOMAIN_EXCEPTIONS}}. Error handling decisions from docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md: {{EH_DECISIONS}}. Error handling patterns from {{ERROR_HANDLING_TEMPLATE}} (exception listener, response factory, status code map). All error handling files live in src/Share/Infrastructure/ErrorHandling/ (cross-cutting, not per-BC). Depends on fixtures goal: {{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}} — integration tests need seeded data to trigger exception scenarios.

section.name: fan-out-hint
section.hint: single-task — exception listener, response factory, and status code mapping are tightly coupled. All components must be consistent.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Implementation check
investigator.pass: Exception listener registered in Symfony event dispatcher — catches kernel.exception events
investigator.fail: Listener not registered, missing service tag, or not catching kernel.exception

investigator.name: Quality gate
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Share/Infrastructure/ErrorHandling/ --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check src/Share/Infrastructure/ErrorHandling/
investigator.pass: PHPStan and ECS both exit 0 — zero errors on error handling files
investigator.fail: Type errors or code style violations in error handling files

investigator.name: Test execution
investigator.pass: Exit 0, all error handling integration tests green — each exception type maps to correct HTTP status, RFC 7807 format verified, no internal details in 500 responses
investigator.fail: Test failure, missing test coverage for exception type, or internal details leaked in 500 response

section.rule: Exactly 3 investigators (GM-08 allows 2-4). Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Domain exception class definitions (built by domain goals), controller actions (handled by action goals), auth flows (handled by auth goals), fixtures (handled by fixtures goal), application-level validation logic (handled by application goals) — error handling goal covers only the cross-cutting exception listener, response factory, and status code mapping infrastructure.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP and record ID
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Implement global error handling: exception listener, RFC 7807 responses, status code mapping
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.21.4 (exactly 8 items, EH-01..EH-08)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.21.5 (4 commands)
CONTEXT string from substep 3.21.6 (purpose + fan-out hint)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.21.6's 3 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 3.21.7
[FIXTURES_GOAL_ID] — the fixtures goal ID collected in substep 3.21.1

substep.rule: Description is 88 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 (G-11) — error handling issues are typically missing listener registration or wrong status code, fixable in few cycles.
phase=error_handling on the created goal.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Record the returned goal ID as ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID session variable — steps 3.18 and 3.19 depend on this.
Print: "Error handling created: goal-{{ID}} (phase=error_handling, depends_on=[{{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}}]) with 3 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3"
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate HTTP Middleware goal (conditional, phase=middleware)
step.rule: Fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs.
This step is CONDITIONAL — if no middleware was selected during discovery, skip entirely with a log message and proceed to the next step.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Check middleware selection in cross-cutting.md
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md. Find the middleware section (written by discover.xml Step 4.2/6.4 — covers CORS, request logging, rate limiting).
Extract which middleware features were selected/enabled. Build SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE list.

branch.condition: SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE is empty — no middleware selected
branch.action: Print: "No HTTP middleware selected — skipping Step 3.22"
SKIP — proceed to next step. Do NOT call goal-create.
Print: "Middleware selected: {{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}}. Generating middleware goal."
Proceed to substep 3.22.2.

substep.title: Resolve dependency goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=fixtures — store its ID as FIXTURES_GOAL_ID.

check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate middleware goal: no fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) found in goals.yaml — ensure fixtures step ran first"

substep.action: Set MW_DEPENDS_ON = [FIXTURES_GOAL_ID].

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (MW-01..MW-04)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array from docs/task-plan-spec.md:725-737, filtering MW-01..MW-03 based on SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE:

criteria.item: CORS configured correctly for specified origins — Playwright E2E: OPTIONS request returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin header
Request logging captures method, path, status code, duration — read log file after test request to verify entry
Rate limiting returns 429 after configured threshold — integration test sends N+1 requests and asserts 429
All middleware registered in correct priority order in Symfony kernel — verify via bin/console debug:event-dispatcher or config inspection

substep.rule: Include MW-01, MW-02, MW-03 only when their corresponding middleware feature appears in SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE. MW-04 is always included (minimum 2 criteria).

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array based on selected middleware:
substep.rule: $BASE_URL references the value from docs/architecture/test-environment.md — never hardcoded (GM-05).

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: HTTP middleware goal: configure and test selected middleware features ({{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}}). See docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md for middleware selection decisions and configuration details. Middleware registration order matters — CORS before rate limiting before logging is typical Symfony priority ordering. All middleware registered via Symfony kernel event subscribers or framework config (e.g. nelmio/cors-bundle for CORS). Middleware files live in src/Share/Infrastructure/Http/Middleware/ (cross-cutting, not per-BC).

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Kernel registration
investigator.pass: All selected middleware appear as listeners with correct priority ordering
investigator.fail: Missing middleware listener or incorrect priority

investigator.name: Integration tests
investigator.pass: Exit 0, all middleware tests green — CORS headers, log entries, rate limit 429 verified
investigator.fail: Test failure or missing test coverage for selected middleware features

investigator.name: Quality gates
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/Share/Infrastructure/Http/Middleware/ --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check src/Share/Infrastructure/Http/Middleware/
investigator.pass: PHPStan and ECS both exit 0 — zero errors
investigator.fail: Type errors or code style violations in middleware files

section.rule: 3 investigators (GM-08 allows 2-4). Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.action: Set NOT_IN_SCOPE to:
substep.value: Domain logic, application handlers, authentication, controller actions, database persistence, fixtures, E2E tests for non-middleware features — all handled by prior goals. Middleware features not selected during discovery are explicitly excluded.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Configure HTTP middleware: {{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}} with kernel registration and tests (≤120 chars — truncate list if needed)
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.22.3 (2-4 items depending on selections)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.22.4
CONTEXT string from substep 3.22.5 (purpose)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.22.5's 3 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 3.22.6
MW_DEPENDS_ON = [FIXTURES_GOAL_ID] from substep 3.22.2

substep.rule: Description dynamically lists selected middleware names — must stay ≤120 chars (MaxGoalDescriptionLength). If list exceeds limit, abbreviate: "Configure HTTP middleware: N features with kernel registration".
max_retries=3 — middleware goals have focused cross-cutting scope.
phase=middleware on the created goal.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Middleware created: goal-{{ID}} (phase=middleware, depends_on=[{{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}}]) with 3 investigators, selected: {{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}}, max_retries=3"
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate API documentation goal (phase=api_docs) — CONDITIONAL
step.rule: This step is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if OpenAPI/nelmio was not selected during discovery.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Check OpenAPI selection in discovery output
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md. Locate the Operational Concerns section.
Search for indicators that OpenAPI/nelmio/api-doc-bundle was selected: keywords "OpenAPI", "nelmio", "api-doc-bundle", "Swagger", or equivalent.

check.condition: OpenAPI/nelmio NOT found in Operational Concerns
check.action: Print: "API documentation not selected — skipping step 3.23"
SKIP entire step — proceed to next step.

substep.action: If found, store API_DOC_FORMAT = detected format description (e.g., "OpenAPI 3.0 via nelmio/api-doc-bundle").
Print: "API documentation selected: {{API_DOC_FORMAT}} — proceeding with goal generation"

substep.title: Resolve dependency goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter all goal entries where phase=action. Collect their goal IDs into ACTION_GOAL_IDS list.
Filter all goal entries where phase=auth. Collect their goal IDs into AUTH_GOAL_IDS list.
Set API_DOC_DEPENDS_ON = ACTION_GOAL_IDS + AUTH_GOAL_IDS.

check.action: ABORT with error: "No controller action goals found in goals.yaml (phase=action) — cannot generate API docs goal without endpoints to document"

substep.action: Print: "API docs depends on {{count(ACTION_GOAL_IDS)}} action goals + {{count(AUTH_GOAL_IDS)}} auth goals"
substep.rule: AUTH_GOAL_IDS may be empty if the project has no auth flows — that is valid. ACTION_GOAL_IDS must never be empty.
This MUST be dynamic — goal counts vary per project. Never hardcode goal IDs.

substep.title: Build acceptance criteria array
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array from AD-01..AD-04:

criteria.c: OpenAPI spec generated at configured endpoint (e.g., /api/doc.json) — curl endpoint, valid JSON returned
Every controller action documented in OpenAPI spec — count operations in spec matches action count
Request/response schemas match actual DTOs — compare spec schemas with DTO properties
OpenAPI spec validates against OpenAPI 3.0 schema — swagger-cli validate or online validator

substep.title: Build CONTEXT string with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with three sections: Purpose, Deliverables, Investigation Config.
Purpose: "Configure {{API_DOC_FORMAT}} for all controller endpoints. Generate valid OpenAPI 3.0 spec with complete annotations."

Deliverables: nelmio/api-doc-bundle configuration (nelmio_api_doc.yaml), OpenAPI annotations on all controller actions, schema definitions for request/response DTOs, OpenAPI endpoint route registration.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: OpenAPI endpoint reachable
investigator.pass: Endpoint returns valid JSON — OpenAPI spec is served
investigator.fail: Endpoint unreachable, returns error, or invalid JSON

investigator.name: Operation coverage
investigator.commands: curl -sf {base_url}/api/doc.json | python3 -c "import sys,json; spec=json.load(sys.stdin); ops=sum(len(m) for p in spec.get('paths',{}).values() for m in [p] if isinstance(p,dict)); print(f'Operations: {ops}')"
investigator.pass: Operation count matches total controller action count (ACTION_COUNT + N_auth_flows)
investigator.fail: Operation count mismatch — some endpoints undocumented

investigator.name: Schema validation
investigator.pass: Exit 0 — spec validates against OpenAPI 3.0 schema
investigator.fail: Validation errors in spec structure or schema references

section.rule: 3 investigators for API docs goal (GM-08 minimum is 2). Each has type, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Call goal-create for API documentation goal
substep.action: Call goal-create MCP tool:

params.param: API docs: configure nelmio/api-doc-bundle with OpenAPI annotations and schema validation
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.23.3 (AD-01..AD-04 — 4 items)
[ "curl -sf {base_url}/api/doc.json | python3 -m json.tool", "vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/ --level=9", "vendor/bin/ecs check src/", "vendor/bin/deptrac analyse" ]
CONTEXT string from substep 3.23.4 (purpose + deliverables)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.23.4's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.

Controller implementation (built by action goals), auth endpoints (built by auth goals), domain/application/infrastructure layers, DTO creation (built by per-BC goals), database migrations, frontend UI.
API_DOC_DEPENDS_ON — ALL action + auth goal IDs collected in substep 3.23.2

substep.action: After successful goal-create, record the returned goal ID as API_DOC_GOAL_ID.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.

substep.rule: description must be <=120 characters (MaxGoalDescriptionLength invariant).
max_retries=3 per G-11.
phase=api_docs on the created goal.

substep.title: Record API documentation goal ID
substep.action: Store API_DOC_GOAL_ID for use by downstream steps.

Print summary: "API documentation goal created: 1 goal (phase=api_docs, depends_on={{count(API_DOC_DEPENDS_ON)}} goals [{{count(ACTION_GOAL_IDS)}} action + {{count(AUTH_GOAL_IDS)}} auth]), ID: {{API_DOC_GOAL_ID}}"
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate Messenger transport goal (conditional, phase=messenger)
step.rule: Steps 3.20-3.23 must have completed before this step executes.
This step is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no async/Messenger selection.
Single goal (cross-cutting infrastructure, not per-BC). phase=messenger. depends_on=ALL event listener goal IDs.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk — never from conversation context (G-16).
Transport DSN is NOT hardcoded — references test-environment.md for connection details and cross-cutting.md for ADR transport choice (GM-05).

substep.title: Check async/Messenger selection from discovery output
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md from disk.
Search for Messenger/async transport selection (keywords: "Messenger", "async", "async transport", "message queue").

branch.action: Print: "Messenger transport not selected — skipping step 3.24"
SKIP entire step 3.24. Do NOT proceed to substep 3.24.2.
Extract transport type choice (doctrine vs AMQP) from cross-cutting.md or ADRs.
Extract list of async messages/events to route through Messenger.
Proceed to substep 3.24.2.

substep.title: Resolve event listener dependency goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk.
Find all goals with phase=event_listener. Collect their IDs as EVENT_LISTENER_GOAL_IDS.
Set MESSENGER_DEPENDS_ON = EVENT_LISTENER_GOAL_IDS.

check.condition: no event listener goals found in goals.yaml
check.action: ABORT with error: "Step 3.24 requires event listener goals (phase=event_listener) but none found in goals.yaml — run event listener generation (step 3.20) first"

substep.rule: Events must exist before transport routing — Messenger transport depends on event listener goals.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (MT-01..MT-05)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array from MT-01 through MT-05 (docs/task-plan-spec.md:757-763):

criteria.item: messenger.yaml has transport configured per ADR choice (doctrine://default or AMQP per cross-cutting.md decision)
Failed message transport configured — `failed` transport present in messenger.yaml
Retry strategy configured with reasonable defaults (3 retries, 1000ms delay, 2x multiplier per context-mapping.md patterns)
`messenger:consume` command works — consumer starts and exits cleanly with --limit=1 --time-limit=5
Routing maps async messages to correct transport — Published Language events from src/Share/Event/ routed to async transport

substep.rule: All 5 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose context with deliverables and Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Messenger transport configuration for async message handling. Transport type decision from docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md (doctrine://default vs AMQP). Routing patterns from .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/context-mapping.md (Messenger Transport Configuration section). Connection details from docs/architecture/test-environment.md — never hardcode DSN values.

section.name: deliverables
section.file: config/packages/messenger.yaml — transport, routing, retry strategy, failed transport
docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md — updated with transport config decisions (if needed)

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Config check
investigator.pass: Transport registered, routing configured, retry strategy present, failed transport defined
investigator.fail: Missing config, invalid routing, or unregistered transport

investigator.name: Consumer test
investigator.pass: Consumer starts and exits cleanly (exit 0)
investigator.fail: Consumer error, transport misconfigured, or non-zero exit

section.rule: 2 investigators — minimum required by GM-08. Each has type, files/commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.section: Event handler implementations, domain event definitions, ACL adapters, infrastructure dispatch logic, production AMQP broker setup — all handled by domain, infrastructure, or event listener goals.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP and record ID
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Messenger: configure async transport, retry strategy, and consumer worker
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.24.3 (exactly 5 items, MT-01..MT-05)
["bin/console messenger:consume async --limit=1 --time-limit=5", "bin/console debug:messenger"]
CONTEXT string from substep 3.24.4 (purpose + deliverables + not-in-scope)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.24.4's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
Event handler implementations, domain event definitions, ACL adapters, infrastructure dispatch logic, production AMQP broker setup
MESSENGER_DEPENDS_ON — ALL event listener goal IDs from substep 3.24.2
messenger

substep.rule: Description is 66 characters — well within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 — single cross-cutting goal with focused scope (G-11).
validate uses --limit=1 --time-limit=5 to ensure consumer exits cleanly in CI rather than blocking indefinitely.
substep.action: After successful goal-create, store returned goal ID as MESSENGER_GOAL_ID.
Print summary: "Messenger transport goal created: 1 goal (phase=messenger, depends_on=[EVENT_LISTENER_GOAL_IDS]), ID: MESSENGER_GOAL_ID"

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.

step.title: Generate Health Check goal (single goal, phase=health_check)
step.rule: This step is UNCONDITIONAL — every project needs a health check endpoint.
depends_on MUST include EVERY infrastructure goal ID — dynamically collected from goals.yaml, not hardcoded (G-03).
Single goal — never split per-BC. Health check is a cross-cutting concern covering all services.

substep.title: Collect all infrastructure goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter all goal entries where phase=infrastructure. Collect their goal IDs into INFRA_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: ABORT with error: "No infrastructure goals found in goals.yaml — cannot generate health check goal without infrastructure dependencies"

substep.action: Print: "Found {{count(INFRA_GOAL_IDS)}} infrastructure goals: {{INFRA_GOAL_IDS}}"
substep.rule: This MUST be dynamic — BC count varies per project. Never hardcode goal IDs.

substep.title: Identify services from discovery outputs
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md and docs/architecture/bounded-contexts.md. Extract infrastructure services present in the project (database, Redis, message queue if selected).
Build SERVICES list containing only the services actually configured for this project.
substep.rule: Never hardcode the service list — derive from discovery outputs. Different projects may have only DB, or DB+Redis, or DB+Redis+queue.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (HC-01..HC-03)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 3 health check criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:

criteria.item: GET /health returns 200 when all services up
GET /health returns 503 when a service is down
Response includes individual service statuses

substep.rule: All 3 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with commands that verify the health check endpoint:

commands.cmd: curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq . (expect JSON with per-service status keys matching SERVICES)

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Health check goal creates a single GET /health endpoint that reports system status with per-service checks for {{SERVICES}}. Depends on ALL infrastructure goals: {{INFRA_GOAL_IDS}} — these provide the services that health check monitors.

section.name: fan-out-hint
section.hint: single-task — one endpoint, one controller, one set of service checks.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Health endpoint response
investigator.pass: HTTP 200, JSON body contains status keys for each service in SERVICES, all marked healthy
investigator.fail: Non-200 status code, missing service keys, or non-JSON response

investigator.name: Degraded health check
investigator.commands: Integration test with mocked failing service — stop one service dependency, hit /health
investigator.pass: HTTP 503, response body shows failed service with unhealthy status while others remain healthy
investigator.fail: Still returns 200 when a service is down, or missing per-service status detail

section.rule: Exactly 2 investigators (GM-08 allows 2-4). Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

section.name: not-in-scope
section.value: Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes, metrics endpoints, detailed performance diagnostics — these are deployment-specific concerns beyond the health check endpoint itself.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Create GET /health endpoint returning system status with per-service checks
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.25.3 (exactly 3 items, HC-01..HC-03)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.25.4
CONTEXT string from substep 3.25.5 (purpose + fan-out hint)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.25.5's 2 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
NOT_IN_SCOPE from substep 3.25.5
INFRA_GOAL_IDS — ALL infrastructure goal IDs collected in substep 3.25.1

substep.rule: Description is 73 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 (G-11) — health check is a single focused goal, fixable in few cycles.
depends_on MUST be the FULL INFRA_GOAL_IDS list — never just the last one (G-03).

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Health check created: goal ID (phase=health_check, depends_on={{INFRA_GOAL_IDS}}) with 2 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3"
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate Docker/deployment goal (conditional, phase=docker)
step.rule: This step is CONDITIONAL — skip ONLY if RUN_TARGET!=docker AND no Docker/container/deployment ADR exists in docs/architecture/adrs/. RUN_TARGET=docker alone fires the compose+ports goal even with no deployment ADR.

DECOUPLED triggers: "run in a container" (RUN_TARGET=docker → always needs compose+ports) is separate from "production deployment" (Docker/deployment ADR → adds prod extras). Either trigger fires the goal; both present emit ONE goal (ADR only adds extras), never two.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.
depends_on MUST include ALL previous goal IDs from goals.yaml — Docker goal depends on the entire project (G-03).

substep.title: Determine compose trigger (RUN_TARGET=docker OR deployment ADR)
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md from disk. Set RUN_TARGET: test-environment.md "Run Target" == docker → set RUN_TARGET=docker, else RUN_TARGET=local. (IDENTICAL rule to substep 1.2/step 0b — do not drift. If test-environment.md is absent, treat RUN_TARGET=local.)

Scan docs/architecture/adrs/ directory. Read all ADR files. Search for Docker/container/deployment keywords: "docker", "dockerfile", "docker-compose", "container", "containerization", "deployment". Set ADR_PRESENT=true if any ADR matches, else false.
Set COMPOSE_TRIGGER = (RUN_TARGET==docker) OR ADR_PRESENT.

check.condition: RUN_TARGET!=docker AND NOT ADR_PRESENT (COMPOSE_TRIGGER is false)
check.action: Print: "RUN_TARGET!=docker and no Docker/deployment ADR — skipping step 3.26"
SKIP entire step — proceed to next step.

substep.action: DOCKER_ADR_SUMMARY is OPTIONAL: only when ADR_PRESENT, store DOCKER_ADR_SUMMARY = summary of Docker decisions from the matching ADR (base image, compose services, port mappings, prod extras). When RUN_TARGET=docker without an ADR, leave DOCKER_ADR_SUMMARY unset — base image/services derive from architecture files (substep 3.26.3).
Print: "Compose goal firing — trigger: RUN_TARGET={{RUN_TARGET}}, ADR_PRESENT={{ADR_PRESENT}} (ONE goal; ADR, if present, adds prod extras)"

substep.title: Collect all previous goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Collect ALL goal IDs (every entry, regardless of phase) into ALL_PREV_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: ABORT with error: "No goals found in goals.yaml — cannot generate Docker goal without prior goals"

substep.action: Print: "Docker goal depends on {{count(ALL_PREV_GOAL_IDS)}} previous goals (all phases)"
substep.rule: This MUST be a full scan of goals.yaml — not filtered by phase. Docker depends on ALL prior goals because Dockerfile/compose must account for every service, BC, and infrastructure component.

substep.title: Read architecture inputs
substep.action: Read the matching ADR file(s) for specific Docker decisions: base image selection, compose service definitions, port mappings, volume mounts, production extras.
Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md for service list and connection strings (GM-05 — never hardcode credentials).

Parse the "Published Ports" block in test-environment.md (the per-service table: Service | Host Port | Container Port | Purpose, persisted by discovery / D1a) into PUBLISHED_PORTS — a per-service host:container mapping keyed by service name.

Set APP_PORT = the host port of the app/web service in PUBLISHED_PORTS. App/web service heuristic: the service whose container port matches the framework's HTTP port; when ambiguous, the service named app/web/php in cross-cutting.md (document the chosen service in goal CONTEXT so the worker picks deterministically).

FALLBACK when the Published Ports block is ABSENT: set APP_PORT = the port from the base URL in test-environment.md, and record a context note that ports were defaulted from the base URL (surface as a RISK in goal CONTEXT).
Set BASE_IMAGE = from DOCKER_ADR_SUMMARY when ADR_PRESENT, else the runtime/framework default recorded in test-environment.md/cross-cutting.md (NEVER hardcoded — GM-05).
Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md for infrastructure decisions (Redis, RabbitMQ, database, etc.) that must appear as services in docker-compose.yaml.
Store INFRA_SERVICES = list of infrastructure services discovered (DB, Redis, RabbitMQ, etc.).

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (DK-01..DK-05)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array from DK-01..DK-05:

criteria.c: Dockerfile builds successfully — docker build . exit code 0

docker-compose.yaml includes each service (app + DB, Redis, etc.) with its host:container port mapping from the Published Ports block (PUBLISHED_PORTS) — read compose file, every service and mapping match
docker compose up starts all services — services running after up
Application accessible at the published host port APP_PORT (from PUBLISHED_PORTS) — curl localhost:$APP_PORT, response received
.env.example lists all required environment variables — compare with actual .env needs

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array:

validate.v: docker compose up -d && sleep 5 && curl -s http://localhost:$APP_PORT/health

substep.rule: $APP_PORT is the host port of the app/web service from the Published Ports block (PUBLISHED_PORTS) in docs/architecture/test-environment.md — never hardcoded (GM-05); falls back to the base-URL port when the block is absent (substep 3.26.3).

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with three sections: Purpose, Deliverables, Investigation Config.

Purpose: "Create Docker deployment artifacts for the project. Dockerfile for application image, docker-compose.yaml for local development with all infrastructure services ({{INFRA_SERVICES}}), and .env.example documenting all required environment variables."
Deliverables: Dockerfile (multi-stage build for production), docker-compose.yaml (app + all infrastructure services), .env.example (all required env vars with placeholder values).

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Docker build check
investigator.pass: Exit 0, BUILD_OK printed — Dockerfile builds successfully
investigator.fail: Build fails — missing dependencies, invalid instructions, or copy errors

investigator.name: Compose services check
investigator.pass: All expected services listed (app + infrastructure services from ADRs)
investigator.fail: Missing services or invalid compose configuration

investigator.name: Application accessibility check
investigator.commands: docker compose up -d && sleep 10 && curl -sf http://localhost:$APP_PORT/health && docker compose down
investigator.pass: Health endpoint responds — application accessible at the published host port APP_PORT
investigator.fail: Application not reachable, health check fails, or services crash on startup

section.rule: 3 investigators for Docker goal (GM-08 minimum is 2). Each has type, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call goal-create MCP tool:

params.param: Create Dockerfile, docker-compose.yaml, and .env.example for deployment
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.26.4 (DK-01..DK-05 — 5 items)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.26.5 (3 commands)
CONTEXT string from substep 3.26.6 (purpose + deliverables)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.26.6's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.

Application code implementation (built by domain/infra/action goals), CI/CD pipeline configuration, production deployment orchestration (Kubernetes, Swarm), cloud provider setup, SSL/TLS configuration.
ALL_PREV_GOAL_IDS — ALL goal IDs collected in substep 3.26.2

substep.action: After successful goal-create, record the returned goal ID as DOCKER_GOAL_ID.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Docker/deployment goal created: {{DOCKER_GOAL_ID}} (phase=docker, depends_on={{count(ALL_PREV_GOAL_IDS)}} goals [all phases]) with 3 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3"
Proceed to next step.

substep.rule: description is 70 characters — within MaxGoalDescriptionLength (120) invariant.
depends_on MUST be the FULL ALL_PREV_GOAL_IDS list — never subset or phase-filtered (G-03).
max_retries=3 per G-11.
phase=docker on the created goal.

step.title: Generate CI/CD pipeline goal (conditional, phase=cicd)
step.rule: This step is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no CI/CD platform selected.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.
depends_on MUST include ALL previous goal IDs from goals.yaml — CI/CD pipeline runs all quality gates that previous goals produce (G-03).

substep.title: Check CI/CD selection in cross-cutting.md
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md from disk.
Search for CI/CD platform selection keywords: "GitHub Actions", "GitLab CI", "CI/CD", "pipeline", "continuous integration".

check.condition: No CI/CD platform selection found, or section explicitly says 'none' / 'not selected'
check.action: Print: "CI/CD pipeline not selected — skipping step 3.27"
SKIP entire step — proceed to next step.

substep.action: Extract CI_PLATFORM from the matched text:

map.match: CI/CD mentioned but no specific platform
map.note: Default to GitHub Actions when platform unspecified — record in context

substep.action: Store CI_PLATFORM and CI_CONFIG_PATH.
Print: "CI/CD platform detected: {{CI_PLATFORM}} — config path: {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}}"

substep.title: Collect all previous goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Collect ALL goal IDs (every entry, regardless of phase) into ALL_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: ABORT with error: "No goals found in goals.yaml — cannot generate CI/CD goal without prior goals"

substep.action: Set CICD_DEPENDS_ON = ALL_GOAL_IDS.
Print: "CI/CD goal depends on {{count(ALL_GOAL_IDS)}} previous goals (all phases)"
substep.rule: This MUST be a full scan of goals.yaml — not filtered by phase. CI/CD pipeline must run ALL quality gates (PHPStan, ECS, Deptrac, PHPUnit, Playwright) that prior goals produce.

substep.title: Build acceptance criteria (CI-01..CI-04)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array from CI-01..CI-04 (docs/task-plan-spec.md:795-802):

criteria.c: CI config file exists in correct location ({{CI_CONFIG_PATH}}) — file exists on disk
Pipeline runs: PHPStan, ECS, Deptrac, PHPUnit (unit + integration), Playwright — read config, all steps present
Pipeline uses same PHP version as project — read config, version matches composer.json
Pipeline fails on any quality gate failure — verify fail-fast or required steps

substep.title: Compose context with deliverables and Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with three sections: Purpose, Deliverables, Investigation Config.

Purpose: "Create CI/CD pipeline configuration for {{CI_PLATFORM}}. Pipeline must run all quality gates: static analysis (PHPStan), code style (ECS), architecture checks (Deptrac), unit + integration tests (PHPUnit), and E2E tests (Playwright). Config file at {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}}."
Deliverables: CI config file at {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}} with all quality gate steps, correct PHP version, and fail-fast behavior.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Config validation
investigator.commands: test -f {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}} && grep -q "phpstan\|PHPStan" {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}} && grep -q "ecs\|ECS\|cs-fixer" {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}} && grep -q "deptrac\|Deptrac" {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}} && grep -q "phpunit\|PHPUnit" {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}} && grep -q "playwright\|Playwright" {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}} && echo "ALL_GATES_PRESENT"
investigator.pass: ALL_GATES_PRESENT printed — config file exists and contains all 5 quality gate steps
investigator.fail: Config file missing, or one or more quality gates not found in config

investigator.name: Pipeline syntax validation
investigator.pass: SYNTAX_OK printed — pipeline config syntax is valid for {{CI_PLATFORM}}
investigator.fail: Syntax validation fails — invalid YAML structure or platform-specific syntax errors

section.rule: 2 investigators for CI/CD goal (GM-08 minimum is 2). Each has type, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).
Investigator 2 commands are platform-aware — use actionlint for GitHub Actions, gitlab-ci-lint for GitLab CI.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call goal-create MCP tool:

params.param: Create {{CI_PLATFORM}} pipeline config with all quality gates
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.27.3 (CI-01..CI-04 — 4 items)
VALIDATE array: ["test -f {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}}", "grep -c 'phpstan\\|ecs\\|deptrac\\|phpunit\\|playwright' {{CI_CONFIG_PATH}}"]
CONTEXT string from substep 3.27.4 (purpose + deliverables)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.27.4's 2 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.

Application code implementation, test writing (covered by domain/infra/action goals), Docker/deployment configuration, production deployment secrets, pipeline caching optimization, branch protection rules.
CICD_DEPENDS_ON — ALL goal IDs collected in substep 3.27.2

substep.action: After successful goal-create, record the returned goal ID as CICD_GOAL_ID.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "CI/CD pipeline goal created: {{CICD_GOAL_ID}} (phase=cicd, depends_on={{count(ALL_GOAL_IDS)}} goals [all phases]) with 2 investigators, max_retries=3"
Proceed to next step.

substep.rule: description is under 120 characters — within MaxGoalDescriptionLength invariant.
depends_on MUST be the FULL CICD_DEPENDS_ON list — never subset or phase-filtered (G-03).
max_retries=3 per G-11.
phase=cicd on the created goal.

substep.title: Print summary
substep.action: Print: "CI/CD pipeline created: goal-{{CICD_GOAL_ID}} (phase=cicd, depends_on=[all previous goals]) with 2 investigators, max_retries=3"

step.title: Generate Makefile / DX goal (single goal, phase=dx)
step.rule: This step is UNCONDITIONAL — every project needs DX commands (make test, make lint, make fix, .env.test).
depends_on MUST include ALL cross-cutting goal IDs — dynamically collected from goals.yaml, excluding phase=gate and phase=scaffold (G-03).
Single goal — never split per-BC. Makefile wraps project-wide quality tooling.

substep.title: Collect all cross-cutting goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter all goal entries where phase is NOT gate AND NOT scaffold. Collect their goal IDs into CROSS_CUTTING_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: Fallback: collect ALL goal IDs from goals.yaml (DX wraps everything). Print warning: "No non-gate/scaffold goals found — using all goal IDs as depends_on"

substep.action: Print: "Found {{count(CROSS_CUTTING_GOAL_IDS)}} dependency goals: {{CROSS_CUTTING_GOAL_IDS}}"
substep.rule: This MUST be dynamic — goal count varies per project. Never hardcode goal IDs.

substep.title: Read test-environment.md for .env.test variables
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md. Extract environment variable names and values needed for .env.test file (DX-04).
Store as ENV_TEST_VARS — list of VAR_NAME=value entries the .env.test must contain.
