substep.rule: Never inline .env.test content in the goal — reference test-environment.md as the source of truth (GM-05).

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (DX-01..DX-04)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 4 DX criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:808-815:

criteria.item: `make test` or `composer test` runs full test suite
`make lint` or `composer lint` runs PHPStan + ECS
`make fix` or `composer fix` auto-fixes CS violations
.env.test file exists with test environment variables matching docs/architecture/test-environment.md

substep.rule: All 4 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with commands that verify DX tooling works:

commands.cmd: make test (or composer test) — must exit 0
make lint (or composer lint) — must exit 0 on clean codebase
make fix (or composer fix) — must exit 0, verify it auto-corrects a deliberately broken file
test -f .env.test — verify file exists and contains expected variables from test-environment.md

substep.rule: Validation must prove all four DX targets work end-to-end — not just that the Makefile parses.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Makefile / DX goal creates developer convenience commands (make test, make lint, make fix) and .env.test for the test environment. Single goal because all DX targets share the same Makefile and wrap project-wide quality tools. Environment variables for .env.test from docs/architecture/test-environment.md (never hardcoded — GM-05). Depends on ALL non-gate/scaffold goals: {{CROSS_CUTTING_GOAL_IDS}} — DX is the final cross-cutting goal and must know what tools/targets exist.

section.name: fan-out-hint
section.hint: single-task — Makefile targets and .env.test are project-wide, not per-BC. One task handles all four DX criteria.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Makefile targets
investigator.commands: make test && make lint && make fix
investigator.pass: All three targets exit 0 — test suite passes, linter clean, fixer runs
investigator.fail: Any target fails, command not found, or Makefile missing

investigator.name: .env.test file
investigator.commands: Verify .env.test exists in project root. Compare variable names against docs/architecture/test-environment.md — all required variables present.
investigator.pass: .env.test exists with all variables from test-environment.md
investigator.fail: File missing, variables missing, or values don't match test-environment.md

investigator.name: Lint/fix cycle
investigator.commands: Introduce a deliberate CS violation, run make fix, verify it auto-corrects, then run make lint to confirm clean output
investigator.pass: make fix corrects the violation, make lint exits 0 afterwards
investigator.fail: make fix doesn't correct the violation, or make lint still reports errors

section.rule: Exactly 3 investigators (GM-08 allows 2-4). Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Create Makefile with test, lint, fix targets and .env.test file
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.28.3 (exactly 4 items, DX-01..DX-04)
VALIDATE array from substep 3.28.4
CONTEXT string from substep 3.28.5 (purpose + fan-out hint)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.28.5's 3 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
CI/CD pipeline config (handled by Step 3.27), Docker/deployment targets, production Makefile targets, IDE-specific config files — these are separate concerns handled by their own goals.
CROSS_CUTTING_GOAL_IDS — ALL non-gate/scaffold goal IDs collected in substep 3.28.1

substep.rule: Description is 60 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
depends_on MUST be the FULL CROSS_CUTTING_GOAL_IDS list — never subset or phase-filtered (G-03).
max_retries=3 per G-11.
phase=dx on the created goal.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "DX goal created: goal-{{DX_GOAL_ID}} (phase=dx, depends_on={{CROSS_CUTTING_GOAL_IDS}}) with 3 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3"
Proceed to next step.

step.title: Generate Final Gate goals (goal-T+3..T+5)
step.rule: Steps 3.20-3.28 must have completed before this step executes.
This step is NOT conditional — final gates always run regardless of project configuration.
Creates exactly 3 goals, each with phase=final_gate, depends_on=ALL previous goal IDs, max_retries=5.
The 3 final gate goals MUST be the last 3 entries in goals.yaml (G-08 compliance).

substep.title: Collect all previous goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Collect every goal ID created so far (from goal-001 through the last goal created by Steps 1-3.28) into ALL_GOAL_IDS list.
This list is the depends_on value for all 3 final gate goals — they are terminal gates that run only after the entire pipeline is complete.
substep.rule: ALL_GOAL_IDS must be dynamically collected from goals.yaml, never hardcoded — the exact count varies per project.

substep.title: Generate Deptrac Full Analysis goal (goal-T+3)
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Final gate: Deptrac full codebase layer dependency verification

[ "FG-01: Deptrac zero violations across entire codebase — vendor/bin/deptrac analyse exit code 0", "FG-02: Domain + Share layer depends on nothing (pure, no framework imports)", "FG-03: Application layer depends only on Domain + Share layer", "FG-04: Infrastructure layer depends on Domain + Share + Application only", "FG-05: Controllers (inbound adapters) import only Application layer", "FG-06: No cross-BC imports in Domain or Application layers", "FG-07: ACL adapters are the only cross-BC touchpoints in Infrastructure" ]
["vendor/bin/deptrac analyse"]
Final gate: validates that the entire codebase passes Deptrac layer dependency analysis after all BC goals, fixtures, actions, auth, and cross-cutting goals are complete.
PHPStan, ECS, unit/integration tests, Playwright E2E, coverage, console boot, schema validation, migrations
ALL_GOAL_IDS
final_gate

substep.rule: Description is 60 chars — well within the 120-char limit.
7 acceptance criteria (FG-01..FG-07) — all Deptrac-specific.

branch.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Final gate: Playwright E2E regression — all tests run together

[ "FG-08: All Playwright E2E tests pass when run together (not just individually) — npx playwright test all green", "FG-09: No test isolation issues (order-dependent failures) — run Playwright tests in random order", "G-08: Last 3 goals in goals.yaml are final gates (Deptrac, E2E, Quality) — verify ordering" ]
["npx playwright test", "npx playwright test --shard=random"]
Final gate: validates that all Playwright E2E tests pass when run together as a full suite, detecting isolation issues that per-action test runs miss.
Individual endpoint tests, Deptrac, PHPStan, ECS, unit tests, schema validation
ALL_GOAL_IDS
final_gate

branch.rule: Description is 59 chars — well within the 120-char limit.

branch.action: Print warning: "HAS_FRONTEND=false — skipping E2E Regression final gate goal (3.29.3). No Playwright tests to regress."
Do NOT call goal-create. Record that E2E final gate was skipped — adjust G-08 compliance check in 3.29.6 to expect 2 final gates (Deptrac + Quality) instead of 3.

substep.title: Generate Quality Sweep goal (goal-T+5)
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Final gate: PHPStan, ECS, test suites, coverage, schema, migrations

[ "FG-10: PHPStan level 9 zero errors across entire codebase — vendor/bin/phpstan analyse exit code 0", "FG-11: ECS zero violations across entire codebase — vendor/bin/ecs check exit code 0", "FG-12: Unit test suite all green — vendor/bin/phpunit --testsuite=unit passes", "FG-13: Integration test suite all green — vendor/bin/phpunit --testsuite=integration passes", "FG-14: Test coverage meets threshold (configurable, default 80%) — coverage report generated, threshold met", "FG-15: No TODO/FIXME/HACK comments in codebase — grep check, zero results", "FG-16: bin/console boots without errors — exit code 0", "FG-17: doctrine:schema:validate passes — exit code 0", "FG-18: All migrations applied cleanly — doctrine:migrations:status shows no pending" ]

[ "vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/ --level=9", "vendor/bin/ecs check src/", "vendor/bin/phpunit --testsuite=unit", "vendor/bin/phpunit --testsuite=integration", "vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-text", "grep -rn 'TODO\\|FIXME\\|HACK' src/ | wc -l", "bin/console", "bin/console doctrine:schema:validate", "bin/console doctrine:migrations:status" ]
Final gate: validates full codebase quality — PHPStan, ECS, all test suites, coverage threshold, and Doctrine schema/migration health after all goals complete.
Deptrac analysis, Playwright E2E, new feature code, refactoring
ALL_GOAL_IDS
final_gate

substep.rule: Description is 67 chars — well within the 120-char limit.
9 acceptance criteria (FG-10..FG-18) — all quality/runtime checks.

substep.title: Record final gate goal IDs
substep.action: Store FINAL_GATE_IDS = [goal-T+3, goal-T+4, goal-T+5] where T+3/T+4/T+5 are the actual goal IDs returned by goal-create.
Print summary:
substep.template: Final gate goals created: - Deptrac Full Analysis: goal-{{T+3}} (FG-01..FG-07, 2 investigators) - E2E Regression: goal-{{T+4}} (FG-08, FG-09, G-08, 2 investigators) - Quality Sweep: goal-{{T+5}} (FG-10..FG-18, 3 investigators) - All 3: phase=final_gate, depends_on={{ALL_GOAL_IDS count}} goals, max_retries=5

substep.title: Verify G-08 compliance
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Verify that the last 3 entries are the 3 final gate goals just created.
Check that all 3 have phase=final_gate.
Check that descriptions match the Deptrac/E2E/Quality pattern from G-08 (docs/task-plan-spec.md line 359).

check.condition: last 3 goals are NOT the final gates
check.action: Print error: "G-08 VIOLATION: last 3 goals in goals.yaml are not the final gate goals — another goal was appended after final gates. Investigate and fix ordering."
ABORT.

check.condition: last 3 goals ARE the final gates
check.action: Print: "G-08 verified: last 3 goals in goals.yaml are Deptrac → E2E → Quality final gates."

substep.title: Load generated goals
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Extract ordered list of all goals with their: goal ID, phase, depends_on array, BC assignment (if any).
Build GOAL_SEQUENCE — an ordered array of {id, phase, depends_on, bc} objects in goals.yaml file order.

substep.title: Validate strict phase ordering (G-04)
substep.action: Define PHASE_ORDER: gate=0, scaffold=1, domain=2, application=3, infrastructure=4, fixtures=5, error_handling=6, auth=7, action=8, event_listener=9. Cross-cutting phases (middleware, api_docs, messenger, health_check, docker, cicd, dx) share rank=10 and are interchangeable among themselves. final_gate=11.
Walk GOAL_SEQUENCE. For each goal, verify its phase rank is >= the previous goal's phase rank (equal rank is allowed for same-phase goals and cross-cutting phases).

check.condition: any goal has phase rank LOWER than the preceding goal (excluding cross-cutting interchangeability)
check.action: Print error: "G-04 VIOLATION: {goal_id} (phase={phase}, rank={rank}) appears after {prev_goal_id} (phase={prev_phase}, rank={prev_rank}) — phase ordering broken."
ABORT.

substep.title: Validate depends_on acyclicity (G-15)
substep.action: For each goal in GOAL_SEQUENCE, verify that every ID in its depends_on array refers to a goal with a LOWER sequence index.
Build adjacency list from depends_on. Run cycle detection (DFS with visited/in-stack markers).

check.condition: any goal depends on a goal with higher or equal sequence index, OR cycle detected
check.action: Print error: "G-15 VIOLATION: circular dependency detected — {goal_id} depends on {dep_id} which appears at or after it in the sequence."
ABORT.

substep.title: Validate per-BC ordering (G-04)
substep.action: Group goals by BC assignment. For each BC, collect goals with phases domain, application, infrastructure.
Verify within each BC: domain goal appears before application goal, application goal appears before infrastructure goal.

check.condition: any BC has application before domain, or infrastructure before application
check.action: Print error: "G-04 VIOLATION: BC '{bc_name}' has {phase_a} goal ({goal_id_a}) before {phase_b} goal ({goal_id_b}) — must be domain → application → infrastructure."
ABORT.

substep.title: Validate controller actions after fixtures (G-04b)
substep.action: Find the fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) sequence index. Find all action goals (phase=action) sequence indices.
Verify every action goal has a sequence index higher than the fixtures goal.
Additionally verify every action goal's depends_on includes the fixtures goal ID.

check.condition: any action goal appears before fixtures or does not depend on fixtures
check.action: Print error: "G-04b VIOLATION: action goal {goal_id} (index={idx}) appears before or does not depend on fixtures goal {fixtures_id} (index={fixtures_idx})."
ABORT.

substep.title: Report success
substep.action: Print: "Ordering verification passed: all {N} goals satisfy G-04 (phase ordering), G-04b (actions after fixtures), G-15 (acyclic dependencies)."

step.title: Escalation bounce — turn an escalation need into a sequenced prerequisite (conditional, phase=generation)
step.objective: When generation is re-dispatched for a SINGLE bounced goal carrying an executor escalation (a missing out-of-scope prerequisite), create that prerequisite as a real early-phase goal and wire the bounced goal to wait on it. This is the runtime backstop for the rare prerequisite that the initial plan (Steps 0–4) did not anticipate — NOT a path the full initial plan ever takes.

substep.title: Detect the bounce mode and gate
substep.action: This step runs ONLY when generation was invoked for a single bounced goal in phase=generation (set by the supervisor's bounceToGeneration after an [EXECUTE:ESCALATE need=…] was routed as a spec-defect). Otherwise — a full initial plan from docs/architecture/* — SKIP this entire step.
Bind BOUNCED_GOAL_ID = the bounced goal's ID (the goal the supervisor re-dispatched generation for).

check.action: Print: "Not an escalation bounce — skipping Step 5."
SKIP to end of flow.

substep.title: Read the escalation need from the newest correction
substep.action: List .tmux-cli/goals/<BOUNCED_GOAL_ID>/corrections/cycle-*.md and pick the NEWEST by cycle number (highest N).
Read it and extract the escalation `need` line (the concrete missing prerequisite the executor escalated). Bind NEED = that text.

check.condition: no corrections/cycle-*.md exists OR no need line found
check.action: Print diagnostic: "Escalation bounce for <BOUNCED_GOAL_ID> but no escalation need found in corrections/cycle-*.md — cannot synthesize a prerequisite." and STOP (do NOT loop; let the SpecRetries drain / human halt take over).

substep.title: Resolve the scaffold anchor
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=scaffold — bind SCAFFOLD_ANCHOR = its ID. The prerequisite depends ONLY on the scaffold anchor (the repo skeleton), NEVER on BOUNCED_GOAL_ID (that would create a cycle).

substep.title: Derive the prerequisite config from the need
substep.action: Derive PREREQ_DESCRIPTION (≤120 chars; MaxGoalDescriptionLength invariant), PREREQ_PHASE, PREREQ_VALIDATE (commands proving the prerequisite is satisfied), and PREREQ_INVESTIGATION_CONFIG (2–4 investigators; types from the Reference Table) from NEED.
substep.rule: PREREQ_PHASE: if NEED concerns authentication / JWT / identity / security config / User entity → phase=auth-bootstrap (reuse the 3.16a auth-bootstrap shape: security.yaml + User entity + JWT keypair as applicable). Otherwise → an early gate/infra prerequisite: phase=gate for a build/config/tooling prerequisite, or phase=infrastructure when the need is a real infra deliverable (an "infra-bootstrap").
The prerequisite is a STANDALONE early-phase goal — it must not re-declare anything the bounced goal owns; it provides only what NEED says is missing.

substep.title: Create the prerequisite goal via goal-create
substep.action: Call goal-create MCP tool:

params.param: PREREQ_DESCRIPTION (≤120 chars)
PREREQ_VALIDATE
PREREQ_PHASE
PREREQ_INVESTIGATION_CONFIG
[SCAFFOLD_ANCHOR]

substep.rule: depends_on=[SCAFFOLD_ANCHOR] ONLY — NEVER [BOUNCED_GOAL_ID] (a cycle; goal-add-prerequisite would reject it anyway).
substep.action: Bind NEW_PREREQ_ID = the returned goal ID.

check.action: Print the error with full context and STOP.

substep.title: Wire the bounced goal to the new prerequisite via goal-add-prerequisite
substep.action: Call goal-add-prerequisite MCP tool with goal_id=BOUNCED_GOAL_ID, prerequisite_id=NEW_PREREQ_ID. This appends NEW_PREREQ_ID to the bounced goal's depends_on (append order is harmless — NextPendingGoal gates on DependsOnSatisfied, so the daemon runs NEW_PREREQ_ID first) and increments the bounced goal's escalation_count.

check.action: Print diagnostic: "Escalation cap reached for <BOUNCED_GOAL_ID> — refusing to chain another prerequisite. Halting; let SpecRetries drain / a human intervene." and STOP. Do NOT loop or retry.
Print: "Escalation resolved: created <NEW_PREREQ_ID> (phase=<PREREQ_PHASE>) and wired <BOUNCED_GOAL_ID> to depend on it."

execution-rules.rule: goal-create MCP: single call per goal with fully validated parameters — never batch or retry without re-validation
All discovery data comes from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16) — never from conversation context or memory
Discovery output files are read-only — never modify them during generation
Template resolution uses two-tier lookup: overlay directory first, then _base fallback — never hardcode template content
Validate ALL consistency rules before reporting errors — collect every error, never stop at the first
Goal descriptions max 120 chars — detailed criteria belong in acceptance and validate arrays
depends_on IDs dynamically read from goals.yaml after each goal-create — never hardcode goal IDs

scope on EVERY goal-create: pass a `scope` array of path globs derived from the goal's Deliverables file paths, per the <scope-derivation> rule in substep 1.7. A single-BC goal emits its BC-scoped dir glob(s) (e.g. ["src/{BC}/Application/**"]); a repo-wide / skeleton / final-gate goal OMITS scope. CONSERVATIVE: overlap OR omitted/unknown scope ⇒ the daemon serializes (never co-schedules) — so omission is always safe, and an explicit author `scope:` overrides derivation. The runtime reads ONLY the persisted scope, never goal.md.

llm.critical-rules: * Step 0 is a blocking validation gate — never generate goals if validation fails * All discovery data comes from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context * Discovery output files are read-only — never modify them during generation * Collect ALL validation errors before reporting — never stop at first error * Single-BC projects skip context-map.md check (D-27) * Step 1 (Gate 0) creates goal-001 with phase=gate — 2-4 investigators, max_retries=5, no dev-deps * Gate 0 checks come from environment-gate.md templates (overlay then base), never hardcoded (E-10) * DB credentials referenced via docs/architecture/test-environment.md only — never inlined (GM-05) * Step 2 (Scaffold) creates goal-002 with phase=scaffold, depends_on=[goal-001] — 4 investigators, 3 fan-out tasks, max_retries=3 * Scaffold reads from templates (php-symfony overlay → _base fallback) and docs/architecture/* — never hardcoded (G-16) * Deptrac {BC}DomainAndShare composite layer uses previo2 bool collector pattern from php-symfony/bounded-contexts.md * Step 3.14 (Domain) creates one goal per BC with phase=domain, depends_on=[goal-002], 3-4 investigators, fan-out by aggregate count (>1 → multi-task with task-0 shared + task-1..N per aggregate), max_retries=3 * Domain goals: PD-01..PD-20 acceptance criteria, private constructors (PD-01), named factories (PD-02), final VOs with equals() (PD-03..PD-05), past-tense events (PD-06), collect-only DomainEventTrait (PD-17) * Zero Infrastructure/Application imports in Domain namespace (PD-09), no Doctrine annotations (PD-13), cross-BC refs via ID VOs only (PD-15) * ACL interfaces in consuming BC's Domain/ACL/ (PD-16), Share types in src/Share/ not duplicated (PD-18, PD-19) * Share type ownership: first BC processed claims DomainEventTrait + base DomainException; subsequent BCs reference but don't regenerate * File-disjoint rule enforced on multi-task fan-out — shared files (DomainEventTrait, base exception, Share types) go into task-0 prerequisite * Step 3.15 (Application) creates one goal per BC with phase=application, depends_on=own BC's domain goal ID (dynamically read from goals.yaml), 3 investigators, single-task fan-out (multi-task only if >6 handlers), max_retries=3 * Application goals: PA-01..PA-12 acceptance criteria, one handler per use case (PA-01), port-only injection (PA-02), zero infra imports (PA-03/PA-04), command returns void/scalar (PA-06), query returns DTO (PA-07) * Application layer does NOT dispatch domain events — flush-then-dispatch is infrastructure's job * Read model interfaces live in Application/ReadModel/, not Domain (PD-08) * Step 3.16 (Infrastructure) creates one goal per BC with phase=infrastructure, depends_on=own BC's application goal ID, 3-4 investigators, fan-out by entity count (>3 → multi-task), max_retries=3 * Infrastructure goals: no annotations on domain objects (PI-03), flush-then-dispatch in write repos (PI-06), real DB in integration tests (PI-11), migrations generated not hand-written (PI-10) * ACL adapters (PI-16) and custom DBAL types (PI-08) are conditional — included only when cross-BC deps or compound VOs exist * Step 3.16a (Auth Bootstrap) is CONDITIONAL — emitted ONLY when N_auth_flows >= 1 (skip entirely on 0). Single goal, phase=auth-bootstrap, depends_on=[SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID], 3 investigators, max_retries=3. OWNS config/packages/security.yaml (firewall/provider/authenticator) + User entity with password hashing + (when uses_jwt) lexik/jwt-authentication-bundle config + bin/console lexik:jwt:generate-keypair. Carries AU-08/AU-09 (relocated from per-flow auth goals) + AB-JWT (conditional on uses_jwt). User entity defaults to src/Share/ if no identity BC exists. Stored as AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID for steps 3.18 (auth_required actions) and 3.19 (all auth flows). * Step 3.17 (Fixtures) creates a single goal with phase=fixtures, depends_on=ALL infra goal IDs (dynamically collected), 3 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3 * Fixtures depends_on MUST include EVERY infrastructure goal ID from goals.yaml — never just the last one (G-03) * Test credentials reference docs/architecture/test-environment.md only — never hardcoded in fixtures (GM-05, FX-02) * Step 3.18 (Controller Actions) creates one goal per non-auth action (G-05), phase=action, depends_on=[own BC infra + fixtures + error handling + all auth goals] + AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID (only for auth_required actions), 2-3 investigators, 5 deliverables (GM-12), max_retries=3 * Auth endpoints (## Auth Flows section) are EXCLUDED from controller action goals — handled by auth step 3.19 only * PC-01..PC-17 encoded in acceptance; PC-07..PC-14 conditional on Playwright availability * Fan-Out paths from api-endpoints.md used directly for deliverable file paths — never derived from conventions * Step 3.19 (Auth Flows) creates one goal per auth flow (N_auth_flows), phase=auth, depends_on=[fixtures + error handling + AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID], 3 investigators, 5 deliverables (security.yaml + User entity NOT declared — provided by auth-bootstrap), max_retries=3 * Auth flows read from docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md Step 6.2 output — one goal per flow (Register, Login, Logout, Token Refresh, Password Reset) * Auth goals carry AU-01..AU-10 (flow-specific) + PC-01..PC-17 (all flows) acceptance criteria * AU-08/AU-09 (security bundle, password hashing) are OWNED by the auth-bootstrap goal (3.16a), NOT per-flow auth goals; per-flow goals keep AU-07/AU-10 + flow-specific AU-01..AU-06 and depend on AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID. AU-05/AU-06 (role access) tested within Login/Register * Auth goal IDs stored as AUTH_GOAL_IDS — all subsequent action goals MUST depend on them * Never double-count auth endpoints in per-BC N_actions — auth counted only in N_auth_flows * Step 3.19a (Seed Default Admin) is CONDITIONAL — emitted ONLY when seed_default_admin=yes in test-environment.md `## Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin` AND N_auth_flows >= 1 (skip silently when not requested; skip + warn when 0 auth flows). Single goal, phase=seed, depends_on=[auth-bootstrap goal; FALLBACK first phase=auth/Register goal], max_retries=2, acceptance SA-01..SA-06, 2 investigators (command-run + no-inlined-secret file-check). Validate runs `bin/console app:seed-admin --env=dev` (NEVER --env=test) and asserts the admin row exists. Credentials referenced by env-var NAME only (admin_identifier_env / admin_password_env) — never inlined (GM-05); never folded into the --env=test fixtures goal; never seeds prod. * Step 3.22 (HTTP Middleware) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no middleware selected. If middleware selected: single goal, phase=middleware, depends_on=[fixtures goal ID], 3 investigators, max_retries=3 * Middleware criteria MW-01 (CORS), MW-02 (logging), MW-03 (rate limiting) are each conditional on that feature being selected. MW-04 (kernel registration order) is always included. * Middleware files live in src/Share/Infrastructure/Http/Middleware/ (cross-cutting, not per-BC) * Step 3.20 (Event Listeners) creates one goal per event listener, phase=event_listener, depends_on=[own BC infra + fixtures] (cross-BC also depends on source BC infra), 2-3 investigators, max_retries=3 * Event listener goals use EV-01..EV-13 acceptance criteria: EV-01..EV-07 always, EV-08/EV-09 conditional on is_cross_bc, EV-10/EV-11/EV-13 always, EV-12 conditional on async transport * Event listeners: integration tests only — NO Playwright E2E tests (spec lines 329-332). Action E2E tests assert HTTP response + direct DB state, not event handler side effects. * Cross-BC listeners: Published Language DTO in src/Share/Event/, ACL adapter in consuming BC's Infrastructure/ACL/, handler in Infrastructure/Adapter/ — imports only own BC + Share namespaces * Intra-BC listeners: handler in src/{BC}/Infrastructure/EventListener/, no ACL needed, event class from own BC's Domain/Event/ * Event dispatch chain: aggregate collects via DomainEventTrait (PD-17) → infra repo dispatches after flush (PI-06) → Messenger routes to handler (EV-07) → handler executes side effect * Event listener goals NEVER duplicate criteria already covered by domain goals (PD-06/PD-17 event naming and collection) or infra goals (PI-06 flush-then-dispatch) — event listener goals test the handler/subscription side only * Step 3.21 (Error Handling) creates a single UNCONDITIONAL goal with phase=error_handling, depends_on=[fixtures goal ID], 3 investigators (implementation-check, quality-gate, test-execution), single-task fan-out, max_retries=3. Records ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID for steps 3.18/3.19. All 8 EH-01..EH-08 criteria are unconditional. Error handling files live in src/Share/Infrastructure/ErrorHandling/ (cross-cutting, not per-BC). * Step 3.23 (API Documentation) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no OpenAPI/nelmio selected. If selected: single goal, phase=api_docs, depends_on=ALL action + auth goal IDs (dynamically resolved), AD-01..AD-04 criteria, 3 investigators, max_retries=3. AUTH_GOAL_IDS may be empty (valid); ACTION_GOAL_IDS must not be empty. * Step 3.24 (Messenger Transport) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no async/Messenger selection. If selected: single goal (cross-cutting, not per-BC), phase=messenger, depends_on=ALL event listener goal IDs (dynamically resolved from goals.yaml phase=event_listener), MT-01..MT-05 criteria, 2 investigators (config check + consumer test), max_retries=3. Transport DSN never hardcoded — references test-environment.md and cross-cutting.md ADR choice (GM-05). Validate commands use --limit=1 --time-limit=5 for CI-safe consumer exit. * Step 3.25 (Health Check) creates a single UNCONDITIONAL goal with phase=health_check, depends_on=ALL infrastructure goal IDs (dynamically collected from goals.yaml), HC-01..HC-03 criteria, 2 investigators (endpoint response check, degraded health check), single-task fan-out, max_retries=3. Services list derived from discovery outputs (cross-cutting.md, bounded-contexts.md) — never hardcoded. * Step 3.26 (Docker/Deployment) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if no ADR in docs/architecture/adrs/ mentions Docker/container/deployment. If selected: single goal, phase=docker, depends_on=ALL previous goal IDs (full goals.yaml scan, not phase-filtered), DK-01..DK-05 criteria, 3 investigators (build check, compose services, app accessibility), max_retries=3. Description 70 chars. Reads services from ADRs + cross-cutting.md + test-environment.md. * Step 3.27 (CI/CD Pipeline) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no CI/CD platform selected. If selected: single goal, phase=cicd, depends_on=ALL previous goal IDs (full goals.yaml scan, not phase-filtered), CI-01..CI-04 criteria, 2 investigators (config validation, pipeline syntax), max_retries=3. CI_PLATFORM detected from cross-cutting.md (GitHub Actions / GitLab CI). CI_CONFIG_PATH derived from platform (.github/workflows/ci.yml or .gitlab-ci.yml). * Step 3.28 (Makefile/DX) creates a single UNCONDITIONAL goal with phase=dx, depends_on=ALL non-gate/scaffold goal IDs (dynamically collected from goals.yaml — exclude phase=gate and phase=scaffold), DX-01..DX-04 criteria, 3 investigators (Makefile targets, .env.test file check, lint/fix cycle), single-task fan-out, max_retries=3. .env.test variables reference docs/architecture/test-environment.md only — never hardcoded (GM-05). Not-in-scope excludes CI/CD pipeline config, Docker targets, and production Makefile targets. * Step 3.29 (Final Gates) creates exactly 3 UNCONDITIONAL goals with phase=final_gate, depends_on=ALL previous goal IDs (full goals.yaml scan), max_retries=5. Three goals for retry isolation: Deptrac Full (FG-01..FG-07, 2 investigators), E2E Regression (FG-08/FG-09/G-08, 2 investigators), Quality Sweep (FG-10..FG-18, 3 investigators). ALL_GOAL_IDS dynamically collected from goals.yaml — never hardcoded. G-08 compliance: last 3 goals in goals.yaml must be these final gates. FG-01..FG-18 all covered with no gaps across the 3 goals. * Investigation Config structure (GM-08/GM-09): Every goal.md Investigation Config section MUST have 2-4 investigators. Each investigator has: type (from Investigation Types Reference Table at spec lines 955-979), paths (file paths to check), commands (shell commands), pass/fail criteria. Conditional investigators include a condition field. Never hardcode investigator definitions — derive types from the Reference Table. * Investigation Config OBJECTIVITY (GM-09b) — convergence-critical: every investigator's pass/fail MUST be decidable from a command's exit status or output alone — an exit code, a numeric threshold (wc -l / grep -c / "must output N"), or a presence check (grep -rl returning ≥1 line). The words "coverage", "covers", "appropriate(ly)", "properly", "sufficient", "demonstrably", "as needed", "where applicable", "present" (as a coverage claim) are BANNED in pass/fail text — they make the verdict a judgment call, which is what stalls the dispatch→validate→correct loop (the validator flip-flops, the implementer gets prose, and the goal burns its code-retry budget re-flagging the same finding). A green test suite is NOT proof a required behavior is tested. When an acceptance criterion asserts that specific behaviors/invariants are TESTED, DECOMPOSE it (next rule); the test-execution investigator's pass is then "suite exits 0 AND non-empty AND every presence-grep returns ≥1", never "suite green with X coverage". * Acceptance DECOMPOSITION (GM-09c): never emit a compound "tests cover: A, B, C, ..." criterion. Emit one ATOMIC, individually-greppable criterion per item, and for any invariant/uniqueness/existence rule emit a pair — (a) enforcing code exists in src/ and (b) a test asserting the violation/throw exists in tests/. Mirror every decomposed item into BOTH the acceptance array AND a matching presence-grep in the Investigation Config + VALIDATE array, so a single missing item can never hide behind a green suite. * Investigation Config delivery (M3): pass investigators via the `investigation_config` goal-create param — NEVER also write a literal Investigation Config markdown heading into the `context` param or a goal.md template. goal-create renders exactly one Investigation Config section from the param (or the deriveInvestigators fallback when omitted); a duplicate produces a doubled or nested section that breaks investigate.xml parsing. * Test-environment reference (GM-05): Every goal.md involving database access, E2E testing, or service connectivity MUST include "See docs/architecture/test-environment.md" in its context section. Credentials, DSNs, and connection parameters are NEVER inlined — always reference test-environment.md. * Fan-out hints (G-17): Every goal.md MUST include a Fan-Out Hints section. Single-task goals state "single-task". Multi-task goals list task-0 (shared prerequisites), task-1..N (parallel work units), with explicit depends_on within the goal. Fan-out hints drive tasks.yaml generation — missing hints cause task planning failures. * Playwright conditional skip: When HAS_FRONTEND=false (from Step 1.2 line 141): skip PC-07..PC-14 in acceptance arrays (Step 3.18.3), skip E2E investigators in Investigation Config, skip E2E Final Gate goal (Step 3.29.3), and print a warning for each skip. HAS_FRONTEND=true preserves full behavior unchanged. * Ordering verification (Step 4): After all goals are generated, Step 4 validates phase ordering (G-04), depends_on acyclicity (G-15), per-BC ordering domain→application→infrastructure (G-04), and controller actions after fixtures (G-04b). Any violation is fatal — ABORT with diagnostic naming the violating goal ID and rule. * Step 5 (Escalation bounce) is CONDITIONAL — runs ONLY when generation is re-dispatched for a SINGLE bounced goal in phase=generation (after an [EXECUTE:ESCALATE need=…] was routed as a spec-defect); a full initial plan SKIPS it. Read the bounced goal's newest corrections/cycle-*.md, extract the escalation `need`, derive a prerequisite {description≤120, phase, validate, investigation_config} (auth/JWT/identity → phase=auth-bootstrap; else phase=gate/infrastructure), goal-create it with depends_on=[SCAFFOLD_ANCHOR] (NEVER the bounced goal — that is a cycle), then goal-add-prerequisite(goal_id=BOUNCED_GOAL_ID, prerequisite_id=NEW_PREREQ_ID). Append order is harmless (NextPendingGoal gates on DependsOnSatisfied, so the daemon runs the prerequisite first). On an escalation-cap error, print the diagnostic and STOP — never loop (let SpecRetries drain / a human halt take over). A worker must NEVER call goal-create/goal-add-prerequisite — these are generation-only (they race the daemon). * Subsequent tasks insert Steps 6+ above the closing comment in <flow>
